t_ch: 这些在我的画中可能会变成一团跳跃的黄色光点，穿插在深浅不一的蓝色背景中。有人说我的画很抽象，但对我来说，这就是最真实的世界。
t_en: In my paintings, these may transform into clusters of vibrant yellow dots, scattered across a backdrop of blues in varying shades. Some call my work abstract, but to me, it's the truest representation of reality.

t_ch: 你觉得呢？艺术应该追求视觉上的真实，还是情感上的真实？
t_en: What's your take? Should art focus on depicting things as they are, or capturing how they feel?

t_ch: 你是如何开始创作壁画的？
t_en: How did you start creating murals?

t_ch: 这个嘛...说来话长。最初我只是个普通画家，画些简单的作品糊口。那时候我总觉得自己的画缺少点什么，但又说不上来。直到有天...我听到阿格莱雅大人的一番话。
t_en: That's a long story... Initially, I was just an ordinary painter, making simple pieces to make ends meet. I always felt something was lacking in my work, yet I couldn't quite identify what it was. That changed one day when I heard insightful words from Lady Aglaea.

t_ch: 那天，我正在街头画素描。阿格莱雅大人从我身边经过，停下来看了看画。她说…具体内容有些记不清了，大概就是——
t_en: I was sketching on the street that day when Lady Aglaea passed by. She stopped to examine my drawing and said... I don't quite remember the exact words, but it was something like—

t_ch: 「艺术能触动人心，唤起对过去的记忆。但更重要的是，它应当让人看到未来的可能。」
t_en: "Art does more than just stir emotions and revive memories. It opens our eyes to future possibilities."

t_ch: 那番话如同当头棒喝。我突然意识到，我的眼睛不是缺陷，而是一种独特的视角。从那时起，我开始尝试大型壁画，用我的方式诉说奥赫玛的历史，也描绘它的未来。
t_en: It was a bolt from the blue. I realized my eyes weren't a defect, but a unique perspective. From then on, I started crafting large murals, channeling my distinct vision to bring Okhema's history to life and shape its future.

t_ch: 你看这面墙，它不仅仅是在讲述过去，也在预示未来。这些交织的蓝色线条，代表着命运的丝线。它们纵横交错，就像我们的生活，充满了无限的可能。
t_en: Take a look at this wall — it doesn't merely recount the past, it also foretells the future. The interwoven blue lines symbolize the threads of destiny, crisscrossing like the intricate tapestry of our lives, brimming with boundless possibilities.

t_ch: 抱歉，我又开始滔滔不绝了。创作对我来说就像呼吸一样自然，有时候会忘记身边的人。你呢？你对艺术有什么看法，你认为艺术应该追求什么？
t_en: Sorry, I can get carried away sometimes. Creating is as instinctive to me as breathing, and sometimes I lose sight of those around me. What are your thoughts on art, and what do you think art should pursue?

t_ch: 哦，还有…你能告诉我，你眼中的世界是什么样的吗？
t_en: Oh, and... could you tell me, what does the world look like through your eyes?

t_ch: 我总是很好奇常人眼中的颜色。虽然我可能永远无法理解，但听别人描述总是很有趣。也许有一天，我能把你的描述画出来呢？
t_en: I've always been fascinated by the colors that others see. Though I may never fully grasp them, hearing people describe them is intriguing. Maybe one day, I could create a painting based on your descriptions.

t_ch: 颜色...它们对我来说只是一种概念。但情感，情感才是真实的...这种灰，应该再深一些…对，就是这样，像深海一样神秘……
t_en: Colors... to me, they're merely a concept. But emotions... emotions are tangible. This gray should be darker... yes, just like that, as enigmatic as the depths of the ocean.

t_ch: 哦，是你呀。我刚刚在思考接下来的一组壁画该如何创作，没注意到你是什么时候过来的。找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Oh, it's you! I was pondering my next set of murals and didn't notice when you came over. Is there something you need?

t_ch: 想闲聊几句，现在方便吗？
t_en: I was hoping to chat for a bit. Is this a good time for you?

t_ch: 当然方便！你想聊点什么？在艺术方面，我知无不言，希望你到时候别嫌弃我话太多了！
t_en: Absolutely, now's perfect! What's on your mind? I can talk about art all day. Hope you're up for it!

t_ch: 可以给我介绍下你的创作吗？
t_en: Can you share more about your artwork with me?

t_ch: 太好了，外人只知道恭维我，那些话我都腻了，正发愁找不到知音呢。
t_en: That's wonderful! I often get compliments from others, but frankly, they've grown stale. I've been longing to find someone who gets it.

t_ch: 虽然我能理解，这算是对我的作品的某种夸奖，因为我画得太真实了，他们才会这么胡思乱想。但我实在无法解释，我的创作究竟是怎么做到这个程度的。
t_en: While I understand that's a form of praise for my realistic paintings, as they spawn such wild imaginations, I can't explain how my creations reach such a level.

t_ch: 在我眼里，奥赫玛的每块石头，每缕微风，都诉说着故事。有时是个古老传说，有时是街头的只言片语，它们都能成为我的创作主题。
t_en: In my eyes, every rock and every breeze in Okhema tells its own story. Sometimes they recount ancient legends, while other times they echo snippets of street conversations — all serving as inspiration for my art.

t_ch: 所以我很注重奥赫玛的历史，在资料收集上花费了大量时间——结果到头来，他们却总说我能看到过去，而不知道我背后有多努力。
t_en: I place great importance on the history of Okhema, dedicating an enormous amount of time to research. Yet, people often think I can simply see into the past, unaware of the extensive effort I put in behind the scenes.

t_ch: 你的眼睛还有治愈的可能吗？
t_en: Is there a chance your eyes could be healed?

t_ch: 你的好意我心领啦，其实很多朋友先前也这么问过我，还到处搜集各地的偏方，可惜这些对黑潮的损害都没有任何效果。
t_en: I appreciate your kindness. My friends have asked me the same and have searched far and wide for remedies, but unfortunately, none are effective against the black tide.

t_ch: 我现在也放弃恢复的想法了，毕竟，缺陷并没影响到我的生活。
t_en: I've now given up on the idea of recovery. After all, this defect hasn't really impacted my life.

t_ch: 我常常想，如果我能像常人一样看到所有的颜色，还会成为画家吗？也许正是我在视力上的缺陷，才让我能看到这个世界真正的样子。
t_en: I often wonder if I would still choose to be a painter if I could see all colors like everyone else? Perhaps it's my visual impairment that allows me to perceive the world's true essence.

t_ch: 我总觉得…要是哪天，我的眼睛痊愈了，就再也创造不出那么多令人心潮澎湃的作品了。
t_en: I sometimes think that if my eyesight were ever restored, I might not be able to produce as many deeply moving pieces as I do now.

t_ch: 你认识阿格莱雅？
t_en: Do you know Aglaea?

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人啊，在画师的圈子里，她是传说般的人物。我当然认识她，至于她认不认识我，那就不知道了。
t_en: Lady Aglaea is a legend among painters. I certainly know who she is, but I'm not sure if she knows me.

t_ch: 毕竟，我与她只有一面之缘。她在得知我眼睛的缺陷后，单独跟我聊了几句，具体内容有些记不清了，大概就是——
t_en: After all, I've only met her once. She spoke to me briefly after learning about my visual impairment. I can't recall everything she said, but it was something like—

t_ch: 「艺术能触动人心，唤起对过去的记忆。但更重要的是，它应当让人看到未来的可能。」
t_en: "Art does more than just stir emotions and revive memories. It opens our eyes to future possibilities."

t_ch: 就这么几句话，却彻底改变了我的人生。我找到了属于自己的创作方式，自那以后，我画画时常常会进入一种奇妙的状态。
t_en: Just a few words, yet they completely transformed my life. I discovered my unique style of creation, and ever since, I frequently find myself in a marvelous state while painting.

t_ch: 冥冥中仿佛有股力量在引导我，让我能感受到那些被常人所忽略的情感与细节。可惜，后来我就再也没和阿格莱雅大人单独聊过了。
t_en: It's as if a force guides me, allowing me to feel emotions and details that others overlook. Unfortunately, I've never had another chance to talk to Lady Aglaea alone.

t_ch: 我没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 哦，你要走了吗？好吧...谢谢你的倾听。
t_en: Oh, are you leaving? Well... thanks for listening.

t_ch: 我还有事，就聊到这儿吧。
t_en: I've got to get going, but it was great chatting with you.

t_ch: 好，以后有空再聊吧。
t_en: Sure, let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 对了，如果你在城里看到什么有趣的景象，也请告诉我。艺术家总是需要新的灵感。再见，我的朋友，愿墨涅塔与你同在！
t_en: By the way, if you come across anything interesting around the city, please let me know. Artists are always in search of new inspiration. Farewell, my friend, and may Mnestia be with you!

t_ch: 门扉五♪，践行二♬，离愁堆一篮♪——
t_en: Five doors open wide, two steps in stride, a basket full of parting sighs—

t_ch: 黄土干♪，黑土软♪，红土炒家常♬——
t_en: Yellow earth so dry, black earth so soft, red earth stirred in a humble fry—

t_ch: 这是什么绕口令吗？
t_en: Is this some kind of tongue twister?

t_ch: 没错，这是大地兽的食谱，做错了可是会导致营养不良的。
t_en: That's right! This is the dromases' recipe, and getting it wrong could lead to malnutrition.

t_ch: 这么讲究？
t_en: That particular?

t_ch: 那是当然，我可以自豪地说，我们家的大地兽，吃得比我好！
t_en: Absolutely! I can proudly say dromases eat better than I do!

t_ch: …咦？哦！我认得你，异邦的勇士阁下！这可真是太荣幸了！
t_en: Oh! I recognize you, great warrior of the outlands! This is truly an honor!

t_ch: 我是克特西，是这些大家伙们的饲养员之一，但我更希望你叫我「大地兽的主厨」！
t_en: I am Ctesias, one of the caretakers here, but I'd prefer you call me "Chef of the Dromases"!

t_ch: 无论「四味烤红土」、「大地排」还是「土包子」！样样手到擒来！
t_en: Whether it's "Four Flavors Redsoil Roast," "Earthbound Delight," or "Ground Buns" — I can whip them all up!

t_ch: 人能吃吗？
t_en: Can humans eat it?

t_ch: 呃，这，能是能，倒是吃不死人，但这土…您应该不会想……
t_en: Uh, you can, technically — it won't kill you, but this is actual soil... you probably wouldn't want to...

t_ch: 让我尝尝！
t_en: Lemme have a taste!

t_ch: 合着您真打算吃啊！没想到除了我之外还有敢尝土的人，既然如此，我就不拦着您了。
t_en: You really were going to eat it! I didn't think there was anyone else who would try dirt other than me. In that case, I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 来，刚出锅的「四味烤红土」，您吃的时候跟我说一声，我别过脸去。
t_en: Here, fresh from the pot — Four Flavors Redsoil Roast. Just give me a heads-up before you dig in, so I can look the other way.

t_ch: 你只是把眼前那块精心烹调过的，优雅的，发黑的——土，送进嘴里轻舔了一口。
t_en: You simply put the elegantly prepared, charred piece of... soil in your mouth and took a small lick.

t_ch: ——一颗超新星在你的嘴里爆发，最终坍缩成黑洞，将一切知觉全部吸入。
t_en: —A supernova ignites in your mouth, only to collapse into a black hole, pulling in every sensation and swallowing all perception.

t_ch: 你即是大地。
t_en: You are the earth.

t_ch: 一段时间后……
t_en: Some time later...

t_ch: 刺激吧？它们就好这口。第七杯水清完，差不多味道就能散去了。这是我的经验之谈。
t_en: Quite a kick, right? They just love the flavor. By the seventh glass of water, the taste should start to fade — that's what I've learned from experience.

t_ch: 还是算了。
t_en: Actually, let's forget it.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，消化不了的。还是不要跟它们抢了。
t_en: Yep, indigestible. Best not to compete with them for it.

t_ch: 它们吃得出区别吗？
t_en: Can they actually tell the difference?

t_ch: 当然啦，自从菜单丰富起来，其他的饲养员都跟我报告说它们更温顺了！
t_en: Oh, absolutely! Ever since we expanded the menu, the other caretakers say they've become a lot more docile!

t_ch: 总之，很高兴见到勇士阁下你！后面要是阁下想投喂大地兽，或者单纯想和它们说说话，都可以来找我！
t_en: It's great to see you, great warrior! If you ever want to feed the dromases or just chat with them, feel free to stop by!

t_ch: 砂土多吃不健康，红土营养刚刚好——舔…这个味道应该是对的，呸呸呸。
t_en: Too much sandy soil isn't good for them. Redsoil has just the right nutrients... *licks* Hmm, this taste seems right... ptooey.

t_ch: 哦！勇士阁下！今天是来看这些大家伙的吗？
t_en: Oh! Great warrior! Are you here to visit these big guys today?

t_ch: 我有点好奇…
t_en: I'm surprised that...

t_ch: 嗯？什么什么？尽管问吧！
t_en: Hmm? What's that? Just ask away!

t_ch: 喂土有特别的喂法吗？
t_en: Is there a special way to feed the soil?

t_ch: 刚见面的时候，我不是在唱一首歌谣吗？那就是简化版的喂养指南了。
t_en: Remember when we first met, and I was singing a little song? That's a simplified feeding guide.

t_ch: 门扉时喂五块，是和它拉近关系，践行时喂两块，是为了让它解馋。最主要的要等离愁时大家都睡去的时候，大地兽们会围一起嘟嘟吃饭，这才是它们的正餐。
t_en: Give them five pieces at Entry Hour to build a bond, and two pieces at Action Hour to curb their cravings. But their main meal is at Parting Hour when everyone else is asleep. That's when the dromases gather together to munch — it's their dinner time.

t_ch: 另外，选红土是因为廉价好找，而干干的黄土和粘稠的黑土对它们来说算是嗜好品。要是哪天它们不开心了，作为调剂，可以混点进去！
t_en: We use redsoil because it's cheap and easy to find, while dry yellowsoil and sticky blacksoil are their favorite delicacies. If they're ever feeling down, you can mix some in as a treat!

t_ch: 整理出这些结论可费了我不少功夫，还得感谢山之民的兄弟们给我提供了不少主意，毕竟吉奥里亚的造物间能实现心有灵犀，说实话，我都有点羡慕。
t_en: Figuring all this out took quite a bit of effort, and I owed a lot to the Mountain Dwellers for their ideas. Creations of Georios have a kind of natural telepathy. I have to admit, I'm a bit envious.

t_ch: 它们只吃土吗？
t_en: Do they only eat earth?

t_ch: 嗯…悄悄说，其实你能吃的东西，它们大多都能吃。吉奥里亚只是给了它们消化大地精华的能力而已。
t_en: Uh... just between us, they can actually eat most of what humans can. Georios just gave them the ability to digest the essence of the earth.

t_ch: 所以在野外扎营的时候，如果你的餐点一回头就不翼而飞，一旁的大地兽却边吃土边往你这偷瞄，不用怀疑，一定是它馋了。
t_en: So if you're out camping, and your meal mysteriously vanishes the moment you turn your back, and you spot a dromas nearby, munching on dirt and sneaking glances at you — no doubt about it. It's got a craving!

t_ch: 不过，还是有些食材会引发过敏，比方说禽类的蛋…具体为什么也不好说，可能是因为吉奥里亚和艾格勒互相不对付吧。
t_en: Still, there are some ingredients that can cause allergies, like poultry eggs... hard to say why. Maybe it's because Georios and Aquila don't exactly see eye to eye.

t_ch: 你对这份工作还真是上心…
t_en: You take your job seriously...

t_ch: 嘿嘿，实话说，小时候，我被大地兽救过一命，那之后就一直在想着能当个饲养员，改善改善它们的待遇。现在算是如愿以偿了。
t_en: Heh, well, to be honest, when I was a kid, a dromas saved my life. Ever since, I've wanted to be a caretaker and make things better for them. Now, I've finally made that dream come true.

t_ch: 嗯？细说救命？
t_en: Hmm? Tell me more about how you were saved.

t_ch: 倒也不是什么大事，我和家人在城外运货时被锋狼袭击，它扑上来的时候，被路过的野生大地兽一尾巴甩晕了。
t_en: It wasn't anything serious. My family and I were transporting goods outside the city when a direwolf attacked us. Just as it lunged, a passing wild dromas knocked it out cold with a swing of its tail.

t_ch: 类似的事件其实还蛮常发生的，要不怎么说大地兽是人类最好的朋友呢。顺便一提，奇美拉没这个称呼，据说是因为它们的交友标准更高……
t_en: Things like this happen pretty often. That's why they say dromases are humans' best friends. By the way, chimeras don't get that title. Apparently, their standards for friendship are way higher...

t_ch: 有成果吗？
t_en: Any progress on your work?

t_ch: 嗯，我做了张地区土质表，详细介绍了各土场的营养价值，还标注了常见威胁，供「寻土人」参考。
t_en: I've compiled an earth quality chart detailing the nutritional values of various soil types and noting common hazards. It serves as a practical guide for Earth Scouts seeking the best spots for their needs.

t_ch: 不过，就因为这事，那个什么树庭的学者们，甚至连元老院都曾经来找过我，问我有没有兴趣加入奥赫玛的探险队。说我的才干当个饲养员有点浪费了…
t_en: Those Grove scholars and even the Council of Elders has approached me because of my work. They've invited me to join Okhema's exploration teams, saying that my talents go far beyond just tending to the creatures.

t_ch: 那可不行，我要是走了，这些大家伙们没人照顾怎么办？
t_en: That won't work for me. Who would take care of these big guys if I left?

t_ch: 总而言之，我热爱这份工作。暂时是不会想着跳槽的，嘿嘿。
t_en: Anyway, I love what I do. I'm not looking to switch things up anytime soon.

t_ch: 不打扰你啦，「主厨」！
t_en: I won't keep you any longer, Chef!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，还真有点不好意思，记得常来找我们玩啊，勇士阁下！
t_en: Heh, I'm embarrassed. Make sure to swing by and hang out with us again soon, great warrior!

t_ch: 不打扰你啦，「主厨」！
t_en: I won't keep you any longer, Chef!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，还真有点不好意思，记得常来找我们玩啊，勇士阁下！
t_en: Heh, I'm embarrassed. Make sure to swing by and hang out with us again soon, great warrior!

t_ch: 你好！唔…是没怎么见过的生面孔啊。
t_en: Hello! Hmm, I don't recognize you. You're new around here, aren't you?

t_ch: 怎么样，在奥赫玛的生活还习惯吗？有没有稳定的收入？千里迢迢来到圣城，可别一不小心把自己饿死了！
t_en: How are you finding life in Okhema? Do you have a stable income? It'd be a real shame if you came all this way to the holy city only to end up starving!

t_ch: 有你这么说话的吗？
t_en: Who talks like that, huh?

t_ch: 嗐，这还不是前几天，传信石版上有人说，有个外地人在客房里蹲了两星期没出门，把自己活活饿死了……
t_en: I'm serious, though. Just the other day, someone on the teleslate mentioned an outlander who holed up in a hotel room for two weeks without leaving and starved to death.

t_ch: 别担心，我喝西北风都能饱。
t_en: Don't worry. I could live on air.

t_ch: 呃…我可不是开玩笑啊，前几天传信石版上有人说，有个外地人在客房里蹲了两星期没出门，把自己活活饿死了……
t_en: Uh... I'm not joking. Just the other day, someone on the teleslate mentioned an outlander who holed up in a hotel room for two weeks without leaving and starved to death.

t_ch: 奥赫玛会饿死人？
t_en: Do people starve in Okhema?

t_ch: 可不是嘛，太吓人了！前几天传信石版上有人说，有个外地人在客房里蹲了两星期没出门，把自己活活饿死了……
t_en: Absolutely. Terrifying, isn't it! Just the other day, someone on the teleslate mentioned an outlander who holed up in a hotel room for two weeks without leaving and starved to death.

t_ch: 说是什么这人初来乍到啥都不会，好心人往他脖子上套了个饼，结果他甚至只知道吃前面的那一小半，都不知道把后边的饼转过来吃！
t_en: They told me a story about a newcomer so clueless that when someone hung a pancake around his neck, he only ate the part right in front of him and never thought to turn it around to eat the rest!

t_ch: 这不是饿死的，是蠢死的。
t_en: That's not starvation. That's just plain foolishness.

t_ch: 我是不是在哪听过这种段子。
t_en: Haven't I heard this joke before?

t_ch: 倒也是一种自然选择。
t_en: It's sort of a natural selection.

t_ch: 或许吧…可你别说，我刚来奥赫玛的时候，因为在黑潮里受了惊吓，也是足足缓了一个星期才敢出门——总是害怕有什么怪物从地里、水里突然就冒出来。
t_en: Maybe... but honestly, when I first arrived in Okhema, I was so shaken by the black tide that it took me a full week before I even dared to go outside. I kept imagining some creature would suddenly pop out of the ground or water.

t_ch: 虽然刚才街上乱成那样…但是和真正的黑潮比起来，反而只能算普通的混乱。
t_en: Though the streets were chaotic earlier, it was nothing compared to the actual black tide. That was just run-of-the-mill disorder.

t_ch: 幸福总是转瞬即逝，不幸才是世间的永恒。就算现在每一天过得平稳安泰，可黑潮要是真的来了…缺胳膊断腿恐怕都是小事。
t_en: Happiness is fleeting, while misfortune endures. Even if days are peaceful now, should the black tide truly strike, losing a limb would be the least of our concerns.

t_ch: 唉，真是不能细想！算了算了，还是打工要紧——{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}满溢之杯{RUBY_E#}保佑，多来点客人吧，只有落袋为安的工资才能令人安心！
t_en: Ah, I mustn't think too much! Work is more important right now... Blessed be the {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Chalice of Plenty{RUBY_E#}. Please bring me more customers. Only a stable salary can truly bring comfort!

t_ch: 传信石版上，总是有些奇奇怪怪的事情啊……
t_en: There's always something strange popping up on the teleslate...

t_ch: 这次又听说了些什么？
t_en: What did you hear this time?

t_ch: {RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的信徒在墓地里载歌载舞、孤儿院修菜窖挖出了一整箱利衡币、有人在投奔奥赫玛的路上遭到不明黑衣人盘问……
t_en: Disciples of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} are dancing and singing in the graveyards, a whole crate of Balance Coins was uncovered in the orphanage vault, and someone was interrogated by a man in black on their way to seek refuge in Okhema...

t_ch: 讲讲墓地的那个。
t_en: Tell me about the graveyard one.

t_ch: 传信石版上的那人说，他是在大家都休息的「幕匿时」看见的。
t_en: The post on the teleslate said the person saw it during Curtain-Fall Hour when everyone else was resting.

t_ch: 他失眠睡不着觉，一时兴起去城郊的墓地散步——本以为这里足够清净，却不成想有一群无法无天的年轻人，跟着自己传信石版上放出来的音乐在跳莫名其妙的舞蹈！
t_en: Unable to sleep, he went for a walk in the suburban graveyard, expecting peace but found a group of unruly youths dancing to strange music from their teleslates!

t_ch: 他有些生气地质问这帮年轻人，对方却说这是祭拜塞纳托斯的仪式，叫什么「涤死课」，让他不要打搅……
t_en: He got annoyed and challenged the youths, who said they were conducting a ritual to honor Thanatos, dubbed "Death Cleansing," and asked not to be interrupted...

t_ch: 他当时觉得自己只有一个人，怕被这些不三不四的年轻人欺负，所以回头去找了墓地管理员，打算一起去轰走他们——
t_en: Feeling outnumbered and worried they might cause trouble, he decided to get the graveyard caretaker to help clear them out.

t_ch: 结果回到墓地那边一看，也就几分钟的功夫，那些不三不四的年轻人竟然完全不见了踪影！地上也没有任何脚印，仿佛一切都没有发生过一样……
t_en: When they got back to the graveyard just a few moments later, the rowdy teens had mysteriously vanished without a trace! Not a single footprint remained, as if the entire episode had been an illusion...

t_ch: 我看有回复说，还有其他人也遇到了类似的事情，不过他们看到的「消失无踪的塞纳托斯信徒」在消失前是摆弄着奇怪的羊头还是什么……挺诡异的。
t_en: Some users reported similar incidents, noting that the "vanishing disciples of Thanatos" were fiddling with odd goat heads or something equally strange... pretty eerie.

t_ch: 讲讲孤儿院的那个。
t_en: Tell me about the orphanage one.

t_ch: 哦，这个不瘆人，是件挺好的事。
t_en: Oh, this one's not spooky at all. It's actually a pretty uplifting story.

t_ch: 说是有所孤儿院在例行修整菜窖的时候，发现菜窖里的一块石板倒了，石板的背后竟然有整整一箱的利衡币，也不知道是谁从前藏在这里结果忘记了。
t_en: An orphanage was doing routine work on their vault when a teleslate fell over, revealing a whole stash of Balance Coins hidden behind it. No one knows who put it there or why it was forgotten.

t_ch: 那家孤儿院本来有些资金困难，没想到孩子们修整菜窖的时候竟然发现了一笔意外之财。
t_en: That orphanage had been struggling financially, yet the kids stumbled upon a windfall while fixing the vault.

t_ch: 有人说这看上去像是传说中的「捷足飞贼」干的，据说那是个身形如闪电般迅捷的怪盗，偷走过许多珍奇秘宝……
t_en: Some say it looks like the work of the legendary Phantom Strider, a mythical thief as quick as lightning, rumored to have stolen many rare treasures.

t_ch: 当然，也有很多人觉得奥赫玛本来就没有什么捷足飞贼，或许这个传闻是个谎言吧。
t_en: A lot of people also believe there never was a Phantom Strider in Okhema. It's all just a tall tale.

t_ch: 讲讲黑衣人的那个。
t_en: Tell me about the man in black.

t_ch: 这个我觉得比墓地跳舞的那个要瘆人！
t_en: Now that one's way creepier than the one dancing in the graveyard!

t_ch: 有个人在传信石版上发辩论场说，他在投奔奥赫玛的路上和家人走散了，结果遇到了一个不太会说人话的黑衣人，把刀剑架在他脖子上问他今天是星期几！
t_en: Someone on the teleslate forum shared that he got separated from his family en route to Okhema and encountered a man in black who was hardly able to talk. He confronted him with a sword, demanding to know the day of the week.

t_ch: 他吓坏了，结结巴巴的告诉对方今天是星期三……结果对方莫名其妙的咕哝了一句听上去像是「还没到吗」的话，就从他身边的山崖上跳了下去！
t_en: Terrified, he stammered out that it was Wednesday. The man in black murmured "Not yet" before leaping off the cliff next to him.

t_ch: 不过我看辩论场上的回复里，没人说碰见过类似的事情——这个段子是纯粹编出来的可能性估计很高。
t_en: No one else has reported anything like this from what I've seen in the forum replies. It's probably just made up.

t_ch: 没什么想打听的。
t_en: Nothing more I'm curious about.

t_ch: 哈哈，传信石版上的东西确实不能保真！
t_en: Haha, you can't trust everything you read on the teleslate!

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 好呀好呀。工作忙是好事，赚钱要紧，哈哈。
t_en: Busy with work, huh? That's great! Nothing beats being productive and making some cash!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，你是不是也想知道我在这里做什么呀？
t_en: Hehe, are you curious about what I'm doing here?

t_ch: 这是我深入研究了{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}为勇者们设立的修炼方式之后，又结合了{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}的仪式，开创出的独门修炼秘笈——「飞瀑潜立」！
t_en: This is my unique training manual, "Flying Waterfall Stance," developed after thoroughly studying the training methods established by {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} for warriors and combining them with the ceremonies of {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 虽然不能做到像万敌哥哥那样刀枪不入，但我会证明，只要修炼得当，奥赫玛人也能拥有不输给悬锋人的勇武！
t_en: Although I can't achieve the invincibility of great Mydei, I will prove that with proper training, we Okhemans can possess courage and strength rivaling that of the Kremnoans!

t_ch: 哟，难得有给悬锋人说好话的。
t_en: Wow, it's rare to hear someone speaking well of the Kremnoans.

t_ch: 是啊，我知道悬锋城有不少坏家伙，伤害了很多其他的城邦…但也有很多好人！像万敌哥哥和他的孤军，愿意保护我们……
t_en: Yeah, I know there are plenty of bad folks in Castrum Kremnos who've caused harm to many other cities... but there are also a lot of good people! Like Mydei and his detachment, who are willing to protect us...

t_ch: 「宁战死，毋荣归」——多帅气啊！
t_en: "Valorous death before glorious return" — how cool is that!

t_ch: 我猜，万敌是你的偶像？
t_en: I guess Mydei is your idol?

t_ch: 那不是当然的吗？无敌又帅气，既是悬锋城的王储，也是奥赫玛的救星……
t_en: Isn't that obvious? He's invincible and dashing, not only the crown prince of Castrum Kremnos but also the savior of Okhema...

t_ch: 重要的是，他还愿意教我怎么锻炼！用什么来称赞万敌哥哥都不为过！
t_en: Most importantly, he's willing to teach me how to train! No amount of praise for Brother Mydei is too much!

t_ch: 只可惜悬锋孤军只让悬锋人加入…不过没关系，万敌哥哥说了，悬锋城有悬锋城的荣耀，奥赫玛也可以有奥赫玛的荣耀…嘿嘿。
t_en: It's just a pity that the Kremnoan Detachment only allows the Kremnoans to join... But it's fine. Mydei said that Castrum Kremnos has its own pride, and Okhema can have its own pride too... Hehe.

t_ch: 怎么样，你要不要也来跟我一起训练？看你人高马大的，直接用悬锋人的锻炼方式也没差！
t_en: So, how about it? Want to train with me? You're tall and strong — you could jump straight into the Kremnoan training regimen without a problem!

t_ch: 所以，悬锋人都练什么项目？
t_en: What kind of training do the Kremnoans do?

t_ch: 什么吃苦他们就练什么！不过，要说最热门的……
t_en: Whatever's the most grueling, that's what they train in! But if you want to know the most popular routine...

t_ch: …应该是高空折返跑！哦，还有无影寻宝、捷足战舞也比较受欢迎。怎么，你有兴趣？
t_en: ...It should be high-altitude shuttle runs! Oh, and shadowless treasure hunting and swift-footed dancing are also quite popular. What, are you interested in?

t_ch: 讲讲高空折返跑。
t_en: Tell me about the high-altitude shuttle runs.

t_ch: 据说在悬锋人的故乡悬锋城，城外深不见底的壕沟上悬着几条碗口粗的巨大铁链子——那些挑战体力与勇气的人，就会在那几条铁链子上玩折返跑。
t_en: It's said that in the homeland of the Kremnoans, Castrum Kremnos, there are massive iron chains, each as wide as a bowl, suspended over the bottomless trenches outside the city. Those who challenge their strength and courage will perform shuttle runs on those chains.

t_ch: 虽然咱们奥赫玛没有他们说的那种铁链，但确实有不少道路都架设在云端。
t_en: Although we Okhemans don't have iron chains like that, a lot of our roads are built high in the clouds.

t_ch: 悬锋孤军…也就是那些住在奥赫玛的悬锋人，就会专门挑这些路线来跑步锻炼。
t_en: The Kremnoan Detachment, also known as those Kremnoans living in Okhema, specifically choose these routes for their running training.

t_ch: 讲讲无影寻宝。
t_en: Tell me about shadowless treasure hunting.

t_ch: 咳咳，严格来说，这个在奥赫玛是犯法的！不过因为不会造成任何严重后果，所以就算被发现了应该也只会被口头警告吧……
t_en: Ahem, strictly speaking, this is illegal in Okhema! But since it doesn't cause any serious harm, even if you're caught, you'll probably just get a verbal warning...

t_ch: 据说过去扎格列斯偷走了尼卡多利的武器，让一场战争大败而归，为了牢记这份耻辱，悬锋人会相约进行训练，一人把对方的物品偷走，然后对方要把被藏匿的物品找回来。
t_en: It's said that long ago, Zagreus stole Nikador's weapon, leading to a crushing defeat in a war. To remember this humiliation, the Kremnoans started a training routine: One person steals something from the other, and the other has to find the hidden item and get it back.

t_ch: 偷东西的人练习的是潜行与保密的能力，找回东西的人则是练习侦察和寻物的本事。万敌哥哥说无影寻宝风靡之后，悬锋城就再也没有小偷了…至少没有活着的。
t_en: Those who steal practice their stealth and secrecy, while those retrieving the items hone their scouting and tracking skills. Mydei said that ever since shadowless treasure hunting became popular, there haven't been any thieves in Castrum Kremnos... or at least, none alive.

t_ch: 讲讲捷足战舞。
t_en: Tell me about swift-footed dancing.

t_ch: 哦，这个最接近奥赫玛本身就有的活动！捷足战舞其实就是光脚跳舞…不过动作比较大开大阖，而且是穿戴好盔甲、全副武装地跳。
t_en: Oh, this is the activity most similar to what Okhema itself has! Swift-footed dancing is actually just barefoot dancing... but with much larger and more sweeping movements, and it's done while fully armored, armed to the teeth.

t_ch: 更高难度的捷足战舞会在点燃的炭火上进行——参与者需要事先淋湿自己的双脚，趁着水没有干，快速地在炭火上跳完所有的动作，然后一鼓作气平安撤离！
t_en: The more advanced version of swift-footed dancing is performed over ignited charcoal — participants must first wet their feet and, while the water is still wet, quickly complete all the movements on the hot coals, then make a swift escape carried by inertia before the water evaporates!

t_ch: 没什么。
t_en: That's nothing.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，不是我自夸，别看悬锋人会吃苦，真论锻炼效果，应该还是我自己发明的这个「飞瀑潜立」最有潜力！万敌哥哥都夸过咱呢！
t_en: Hehe, not to brag, but even though the Kremnoans endure a lot of hardship, I'd say the training method I invented, Flying Waterfall Stance, has the most potential! Even Brother Mydei has praised it!

t_ch: 我还有事，不打扰了。
t_en: I have things to do, so I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 看来{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}你也是个大忙人啊！没办法啦，那我还是自己一个人练吧。
t_en: It seems like you're a busy person too, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}! I guess I'll just practice on my own then.

t_ch: 哎呀…他这个肱二头肌…感觉能把我夹死……
t_en: Oh my... their biceps... feels like they could crush me to death...

t_ch: 啧啧啧…啧啧啧…他这个臀部已经是翘到可以顶一瓶石榴水……
t_en: Tsk, tsk, tsk... and their glutes... round enough to balance a bottle of pomegranate juice...

t_ch: 眼前的男人鬼鬼祟祟躲在屏风后，念念有词地望着远处的浴池，却迟迟不肯下水。
t_en: The man before you is sneakily hiding behind the screen, mumbling something while staring at the distant bath, yet he hesitates to get in.

t_ch: 鬼鬼祟祟干什么呢！
t_en: What are you sneaking around for!?

t_ch: 来人，这有变态！
t_en: Guards, there's a creep here!

t_ch: 哪儿冒出来的，吓我一跳。公共场所，你这话可不兴乱说的。
t_en: Where did you come from? You scared me! Hey, you can't just shout things like that in a public place!

t_ch: 我我我只是因为…浴池的温度还没到位。对，我天生体寒，需要等那水热热再下去。
t_en: I—I—I was just... waiting for the bath to reach the right temperature. Yes, that's it! I was born with a naturally cold constitution and need the water to be warmer before I can get in.

t_ch: 慌乱的神色、结巴的话语、拙劣的借口，出卖了他的心虚。为了翁法罗斯民众的安危，正义的球棒侠决定问个水落石出。
t_en: The panicked expression, stammering words, and clumsy excuses betray his guilt. For the safety of the people of Amphoreus, the Galactic Baseballer of justice decides to get to the bottom of it.

t_ch: 我带你去高温浴池。
t_en: I'll take you to the high-temperature bath.

t_ch: 喔，你人还怪好的咯。
t_en: Oh... you're surprisingly kind.

t_ch: 但这个高温浴池，它太脏了…我皮肤娇嫩，可受不住这种。
t_en: But the high-temperature bath... it's too dirty. My delicate skin can't handle that kind of thing.

t_ch: 我借你私人浴池。
t_en: I'll lend you a private bath.

t_ch: 为何如此坚持，你这样，我怎么好意思开口拒绝…其实…我有脚气！
t_en: Why are you being so insistent? With you like this, how can I say no...? Actually... I have athlete's foot!

t_ch: 对，脚气，嘿嘿，你们不会希望闻到我脱鞋的。
t_en: Yes, athlete's foot, hehe! You wouldn't want to smell it when I take my shoes off!

t_ch: 我有倒立泡澡机。
t_en: I have an upside-down bath machine.

t_ch: 我允许你穿鞋进去！
t_en: I'll allow you to wear your shoes in the bath!

t_ch: 烦死了！！！还有完没完？
t_en: Enough already!!! Are you done or what?

t_ch: 你和他们都是一路货色，开口闭口就是入浴、泡澡，恨不得吃喝拉撒睡都在水里！
t_en: You and them are cut from the same cloth, constantly talking about bathing and soaking, as if you want to eat, drink, relieve yourselves, and sleep all in the water!

t_ch: 嘴上嚷嚷着翁法罗斯人传承浴场文化义不容辞，我看你们呀，只是想趁机秀出你们的健身成果，享受旁人的垂涎。
t_en: You keep going on about how the people of Amphoreus are duty-bound to uphold the heritage of bath culture, but I see through you all — you just want to show off your fitness gains and enjoy the envy of others.

t_ch: 不过不是我说，你这肌肉也练得太差了吧。就你这小身板，就不怕下水后被人耻笑吗？
t_en: Still, not to be rude, but... your muscles are pretty unimpressive. With a physique like that, aren't you worried about being laughed at when you get in the water?

t_ch: 啊？这是我该考虑的事吗？
t_en: Huh? Is that something I need to worry about?

t_ch: 唔！你的这份迟钝…这份厚脸皮…真令人羡慕。
t_en: Hmph! Your obliviousness... your shamelessness... how enviable.

t_ch: 我不被主流审美绑架。
t_en: I don't let mainstream beauty standards define me.

t_ch: 唔！你的这份洒脱…这份自信…真令人羡慕。
t_en: Hmph! Your carefree attitude... your confidence... how enviable.

t_ch: 我有充气肌肉衣。
t_en: I've got an inflatable muscle suit.

t_ch: 狡诈！我可不用这种小人手段。
t_en: Cunning! I'd never stoop to such petty tricks.

t_ch: 每次踏入浴场，我就如同在锻炼时那样，能感到露出我这细胳膊细腿时的恐慌。
t_en: Every time I step into the bath, it feels like stepping into the gym again — I can sense the anxiety of exposing my skinny arms and legs.

t_ch: 而那些强健的青年们闲庭信步，对周遭的歆羡装作不在乎，却又刻意摆出媲美雕塑的优美姿势。人坐在水里，胳膊展得老开，生怕别人看不清他胸肌的轮廓。
t_en: Meanwhile, the muscular young men stroll around casually, feigning indifference to the admiration of others, yet deliberately striking poses as elegant as sculptures. They sit in the water with their arms spread wide, as if afraid no one will notice the contours of their chiseled chests.

t_ch: 而我，无论如何努力抡动手上的重物，收获的却是贫瘠的肌肉与嗤嗤的笑声。
t_en: But no matter how hard I swing those heavy weights, all I gain are scrawny muscles and mocking laughter.

t_ch: 我很好奇…
t_en: I'm curious...

t_ch: 想问什么就说吧，别在我伤口上撒盐就好……
t_en: Feel free to ask whatever you want. Just don't rub salt into my wounds...

t_ch: 我先走了，别太难过哈。
t_en: I'll be going now. Don't be too upset, okay?

t_ch: 我得再等会儿，看他们什么时候走。哎，都好一阵子没洗澡了。
t_en: I'll wait a bit longer to see when they leave. Ugh, it's been a while since I've taken a bath.

t_ch: 差不多就得了，别练了，别练了。练得这么好，就是为了让我感到羞辱么？
t_en: That's enough. Stop training already. You've gotten so good. Is it just to make me feel humiliated?

t_ch: 我很好奇…
t_en: I'm curious...

t_ch: 想问什么就说吧，别在我伤口上撒盐就好……
t_en: Feel free to ask whatever you want. Just don't rub salt into my wounds...

t_ch: 健身为什么会流行？
t_en: Why has fitness become so popular?

t_ch: 这可不是一时的风尚，也不是奥赫玛的专属，而是翁法罗斯所有城邦的通病，只是不同地方的狂热程度有高有低。
t_en: This is not just a passing trend, nor is it exclusive to Okhema. It is a common affliction in all the city-states of Amphoreus, though the level of fanaticism varies from place to place.

t_ch: 在以前，翁法罗斯还很和平的时代，悬锋城会定期举办祭典，健身也是人气很高的竞技大类呢，听说优胜者会受到贵族女子的青睐呢。
t_en: In the peaceful days of old, Amphoreus used to hold regular festivals in Castrum Kremnos, where fitness competitions were also a popular event. I heard that the winners would even gain the favor of aristocratic ladies.

t_ch: 你问大家迷恋健身的原因？这么深奥的事情我哪答得上来，我又不是什么历史学家。
t_en: You're asking why everyone is so obsessed with fitness? That's too profound for me to answer — I'm not a historian.

t_ch: 搞不好啊，都是泰坦起的头。看到天边的刻法勒了吗，那强健的体魄，惊人的力量，我估计它也没少练。
t_en: It might all have started with the Titans. Do you see Kephale on the horizon? That strong physique, that incredible strength — I bet they've put in a lot of training as well.

t_ch: 你也好好练啊。
t_en: You should train too.

t_ch: 我才不要，健身鼓捣出来的死肌肉才没用呢。
t_en: I don't want to. Those muscles from fitness training are useless.

t_ch: 等这些哥们上战场就知道了，肌肉需要消耗更多的能量，没有脂肪储备的身材不抗寒、不耐打、不扛饿，一打一个不吱声。
t_en: Once these guys get to the battlefield, they'll understand. Muscles consume more energy, and without fat reserves, the body can't withstand the cold, can't take hits, and can't go without food. One hit and they'll go down without a sound.

t_ch: 我看你就是单纯地酸。
t_en: I think you're just jealous.

t_ch: 我、我哪有羡慕他们！我正在准备树庭的考试，我要成为学者，才不要做这种肌肉发达，头脑空空的人！
t_en: I—I'm not jealous of them! I'm preparing for the Grove exams. I want to become a scholar, not someone with big muscles and an empty head!

t_ch: 我先走了，别太难过哈。
t_en: I'll be going now. Don't be too upset, okay?

t_ch: 我得再等会儿，看他们什么时候走。哎，都好一阵子没洗澡了。
t_en: I'll wait a bit longer to see when they leave. Ugh, it's been a while since I've taken a bath.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，我看你身强体健，有没有兴趣来给我的发明当测试员啊？
t_en: {F#Girl}{M#Boy}, you look strong and healthy. Would you be interested in testing my invention?

t_ch: 面前的人向你搭话，他一侧的胳膊被一只栩栩如生的义手代替，看上去十分独特。
t_en: The person in front of you strikes up a conversation. One of their arms has been replaced with an extremely lifelike prosthetic — it looks very unique.

t_ch: 你也做了个模拟宇宙？
t_en: You also made a simulated universe?

t_ch: …你说的这「宇宙」是个什么？我不太明白…但恐怕和你想的不太一样，我来给你讲讲。
t_en: ...What do you mean by "universe"? I don't quite understand... but it's probably not what you think. Let me explain.

t_ch: 不是什么怪东西吧！
t_en: It's not something strange, right?

t_ch: 哈哈，我不知道你想到了什么，但不用担心，只是个运动用的辅助装具罢了。
t_en: Haha, I don't know what you're thinking, but don't worry. It's just a support device for exercise.

t_ch: 那要取决于报酬给多少了。
t_en: Well, that depends on how much the reward is.

t_ch: 哈哈，真是个务实的年轻人，但实不相瞒，这方面可能会多少让你失望。先听听情况如何？
t_en: Haha, you're quite the pragmatic youngster. But to be honest, this might not meet your expectations. How about we first see what the situation is?

t_ch: 我发明了一双「腿」，穿上它们，哪怕是腿脚不便的人，也能够像扎格列斯的黄金裔一样健步如飞，甚至能一跃千里。不用我多说，你应该能理解它的价值。
t_en: I invented a pair of "legs." Once worn, even those with limited mobility can move swiftly like the Chrysos Heirs of Zagreus. They can even leap great distances. You don't need me to elaborate. I'm sure you can understand their value.

t_ch: 剩下的就是进一步测试，得找位有活力的年轻人帮我测测，孩子，你乐见帮我这个忙吗？
t_en: Now, the rest is just further testing, and I need a young, energetic person to help me out. Kid, would you like to lend me a hand with this?

t_ch: 我最喜欢测东西了！
t_en: I love testing things!

t_ch: 好，好，真是个亲切的孩子。放心，不会占用你太多时间。
t_en: Ah, good. You're such a friendly kid. Don't worry, it won't take up too much of your time.

t_ch: 你们找到了一处空地，按照博莱斯的指示，你需要随便运动运动，那么——
t_en: You find an open area, and following Bolaris' instructions, you begin to move around... so —

t_ch: 跑两圈
t_en: Run two laps.

t_ch: 你绕着浴场开始跑圈，1！2！1！到家！…怎么卫士们也跟着你跑过来了？
t_en: You start running laps around the baths — 1! 2! 1! Home! ...Wait, why are the guards following you now?

t_ch: 速度不错，孩子，但你身后跟的这群人是怎么回事？
t_en: Nice speed, kid, but what's with the crowd trailing behind you?

t_ch: 啊？博莱斯先生？您是在测试发明吗？刚看见这位勇士跑的飞快，我们还以为是在抓贼呢！
t_en: Huh? Mr. Bolaris? Are you testing your invention? We just saw this warrior running so fast, and we thought they were chasing a thief!

t_ch: 蹦两下
t_en: Jump twice.

t_ch: 你跳了足足十米高！只是落地时刚好有个客人路过，你差点砸到他。
t_en: You jump a full ten meters high! But as you land, a guest happens to walk by, and you nearly crash into them.

t_ch: 我的刻法勒啊！你是从哪蹦出来的？天神，天神降临了！
t_en: By Kephale! Where did you jump out from? A god! A god has descended!

t_ch: 好啊，看来这装置成功发挥出了它的效用……
t_en: Well, looks like this device is working just as intended...

t_ch: 用「开拓」的力气往前跳！
t_en: With the force of trailblazing, jump forward!

t_ch: 你奋力跃向远方，差不多跳了…<unbreak>50</unbreak>米左右吧。
t_en: You leap toward the distance with all your might, jumping about... <unbreak>50</unbreak> meters or so.

t_ch: 艾格勒在上，我刚刚说的「一跃千里」只是个广告宣传啊！
t_en: By Aquila, I was only using the term "great distances" for advertising purposes!

t_ch: 孩子，转身的时候千万轻一些！别把墙撞出个洞来！
t_en: Kid, be careful when you turn around! Don't crash into the wall and make a hole!

t_ch: 在你的帮助下，这位发明家进行了各种测试，一段时间后——
t_en: With your help, the inventor carried out all sorts of tests and, after some time...

t_ch: 孩子，我这装置的升级先不提，这数据告诉我，你可能是一位黄金裔……
t_en: Kid, let's put aside the upgrade of this device for now, but the data tells me that you might be a Chrysos Heir...

t_ch: 虽然不能断言，但至少体能的水平十分相近。如此独特的样本一百个光历年也难遇一例…孩子，这是个好事，但也要注重休息，别太勉强自己的身体喽……
t_en: Although I can't say for certain, at least your level of physical ability is very similar to them. Such a unique specimen is rare, only one in a hundred Light Calendar years... Kid, this is a good thing, but you should also pay attention to rest and not push your body too hard...

t_ch: 无论是民众还是黄金裔，都难以做到完美无瑕，缺憾一词与我们如影随形。
t_en: Whether it's the common people or the Chrysos Heirs, perfection is hard to achieve. The word "flaw" follows us like a shadow.

t_ch: 人们应当告诉彼此，这些缺憾绝非过失所致。而我要做的，就是用我的才智倾力相助。
t_en: People should tell each other that these flaws are not the result of mistakes. What I must do is use my intelligence to offer my utmost assistance.

t_ch: 博莱斯检视着自己栩栩如生的义肢，留意到了你的到来后，望向了你这边。
t_en: While inspecting his lifelike prosthetic, Bolaris notices your arrival and looks over at you.

t_ch: 原谅我这话多的毛病，孩子。是来找大叔我的吗？
t_en: Forgive me for getting so carried away talking, child. Are you here to see me?

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 好哇…能跟你这样有活力的年轻人多打打交道，可是大叔我的荣幸。
t_en: Of course... Being able to talk with an energetic youngster like yourself is a true honor for an old man like myself.

t_ch: 你刚刚说义肢…
t_en: You just mentioned prosthetics...

t_ch: 呵，几十年前，这条胳膊被尼卡多利的眷属抢跑了去，留在了拉冬城里。还多亏一位拉冬勇士恰好在附近，不然现在站这跟你说话的就是别人了。
t_en: Heh, decades ago, this arm was snatched away by Nikador's kin and left in Ladon City. It was only thanks to a warrior from Ladon being nearby that I can stand here and talk today.

t_ch: 那位勇士不厌其烦地跟我讲艾格勒的故事，说永夜也是因为它的眼睛睁不开，让我看开些。后来才知道，那位勇士也在战争中掉了条腿，接了个粗糙的假肢。
t_en: That warrior tirelessly told me the story of Aquila, saying that the evernight was due to their eyes that couldn't be kept open, advising me to take it easy. Later, I learned that the warrior had also lost a leg in the war and replaced it with a crude prosthetic.

t_ch: 那时起我就开始研究各种小物件，给自己做，给邻人做，没多久就造了这手，也给他打了个新假腿，还有空就往天上瞧，等着什么时候问问艾格勒——
t_en: Since then, I began researching various little gadgets, making them for myself and for my neighbors. Before long, I created this hand and even crafted a new prosthetic leg for him. Whenever I had the chance, I'd gaze at the sky, waiting for the moment to ask Aquila —

t_ch: 再后来…唉，他在拉冬城毁时丢了命，给他打的腿到头来也没怎么能派上用场，但我这造东西的习惯，倒是养下来了。
t_en: Later on... *sigh* he lost his life when Ladon city was destroyed. The prosthetic leg he received didn't end up being of much use, but the habit of creating things, well, that stayed with me.

t_ch: 你都发明过什么？
t_en: What have you invented?

t_ch: 主要是以小玩意居多…你很好奇，是不是？好，好，我来讲讲。
t_en: Mostly small trinkets... You're curious, aren't you? Well, well, let me tell you about them.

t_ch: 拿着浴场来说吧，「能稳稳浮在水上不会晃的桌子」、「靠颜色变化来辨别水温的墙砖」、「几秒就能把水吸干的浴巾」…
t_en: Take the bath, for example: "A table that can float steadily on the water without wobbling," "Wall tiles that change color to indicate water temperature," "A towel that can dry off in just a few seconds"...

t_ch: 怎么样，我没说错吧？都是些不值一提的小东西罢了。
t_en: How about those? I wasn't wrong, was I? Just some insignificant little things.

t_ch: 不错啊！还有别的吗？
t_en: Not bad! Do you have any others?

t_ch: 呵，能被你这样有活力的年轻人欣赏，大叔我有点开心。
t_en: Hah, being admired by an energetic youngster like yourself... I must admit this has made me smile.

t_ch: 其他的…「壁画颜料改良」、「大地兽的辅助鞍具」、「石板架」、「云石天宫的吉祥物娃娃」……
t_en: Other creations... "Improved mural paints," "Support saddles for dromases," "Slate racks," "Mascot dolls for the Marmoreal Palace..."

t_ch: 我有点记不太全了。毕竟和失败的尝试比起来，成功的只有一小部分而已…
t_en: I can't quite remember all of it. After all, compared to the failed attempts, there were only a few successes...

t_ch: 那有什么「大发明」吗？
t_en: Any "great inventions"?

t_ch: 大的…大的啊…说起来还真有那么一个，但，唉…我都有点不太愿意提。
t_en: A big one... a really big one... Now that you mention it, there is one, but... *sigh*... I'm almost reluctant to bring it up.

t_ch: 在遗迹里偶尔能看到的那只大手，「蛊惑之手」，把那东西拿来用这事，是我出的主意，后来联合着工匠们一起，捣鼓了几个月，就给做成了。
t_en: The giant hand occasionally seen in the ruins, the "Bewitching Hands." It was my idea to use that thing. Later, together with the craftsmen, we tinkered with it for a few months and finally made it.

t_ch: 但再怎么说那也是扎格列斯的物件，听说总有人拿它做些偷鸡摸狗上房掀瓦的事。久了，那东西惹出来的麻烦越来越多，老头子我又无能为力，愁啊。
t_en: But no matter how you put it, that was an artifact of Zagreus. I heard that some people have been using it for all sorts of sneaky, underhanded deeds — climbing walls, stealing chickens, you name it. Over time, the trouble it caused kept piling up, and here I am, an old man, powerless to do anything about it. It's really troubling.

t_ch: 后来啊，这手被个商人圈了去当商品，还跟我提了一大堆稀奇古怪的要求，我就整个不想管了…帮我个忙，孩子，以后要有人问起来，你可别跟他们讲说是我造的。
t_en: Later on, that hand was taken by a merchant to be sold as a commodity. He made all sorts of strange and ridiculous requests, and I just couldn't be bothered anymore... Do me a favor, kid. If anyone asks about it in the future, don't tell them I made it.

t_ch: 最近在造什么？
t_en: What have you been creating recently?

t_ch: 除了那件辅助装具之外，我还在研究人的眼睛，想着什么时候摸透了，能做个真正能看见东西的假眼睛，但总也不顺利……
t_en: In addition to that support device, I've also been studying the human eye, hoping that one day I'll figure it out and create a real prosthetic eye that can actually see, but it's always been unsuccessful...

t_ch: 这项发明的出资人劝我不要急，他说，很多事都在所难免，况且他都已经习惯了，再者——
t_en: The sponsor of this invention advised me not to rush. He said that many things are inevitable, and besides, he's already gotten used to it. Furthermore —

t_ch: 「即使{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}无法出让它的天空，{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}吉奥里亚{RUBY_E#}也赐予了我们大地兽作为补偿。」——道理是这个道理。
t_en: "Even if {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#} cannot give us ownership of their sky, {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Georios{RUBY_E#} has given us dromases as compensation." That is the logic.

t_ch: 话虽如此，能早日解决始终还是更好。哎，这东西可就不能麻烦孩子你了，哈哈。希望在我变成老骨头前能拿出点成果吧。
t_en: That being said, problems like this are always better solved sooner than later. Ah, I cannot trouble a child like yourself with such things, haha. I just hope that I can make some progress before I am too old.

t_ch: 不打扰啦，下次见。
t_en: I'll leave you to it. See you soon.

t_ch: 好，好，真有礼貌。下次见，愿艾格勒注视着你，孩子。
t_en: Good, good, such courtesy. Until next time, may Aquila watch over you, child.

t_ch: 不打扰啦，下次见。
t_en: I'll leave you to it. See you soon.

t_ch: 好，好，真有礼貌。下次见，愿艾格勒注视着你，孩子。
t_en: Good, good, such courtesy. Until next time, may Aquila watch over you, child.

t_ch: 我看你就是想破坏我们家的声誉！亏我一直以来还当你是好兄弟，真是知人知面不知心！
t_en: I think you're just trying to ruin our family's reputation! I always thought of you as a good brother, but turns out I was wrong! You can't judge a person just by their face!

t_ch: …都说了我兄长不可能做那样的事，况且真论起来，还是你们的嫌疑更大一些吧？
t_en: ...I've already told you. My brother could never do such a thing. Besides, if we're being honest, your suspicion is far greater than ours, isn't it?

t_ch: 什——你怎么敢如此污蔑我，是不是想打架！
t_en: What—? How dare you slander me like that? Are you looking for a fight!?

t_ch: 明明是你挑衅在先，现在还好意思乱发脾气……
t_en: You were the one who provoked me first, and now you have the nerve to lose your temper...

t_ch: 眼见两人间火药味越来越浓，你聚精会神地围观着，生怕一不小心错过战斗开始的精彩瞬间。
t_en: As the tension between the two keeps building, you watch closely, not wanting to miss the exciting moment when the fight kicks off.

t_ch: 哈哈…这位朋友，你看得还挺津津有味的嘛~那想不想知道他们究竟在吵什么呀？
t_en: Haha... Friend, you seem quite intrigued. Would you like to know what exactly they're arguing about?

t_ch: 只需要区区<unbreak>50</unbreak>利衡币，前因后果包给你讲清楚。
t_en: It only takes <unbreak>50</unbreak> Balance Coins. I'll explain everything, including the background, clearly to you.

t_ch: 行吧…
t_en: Alright...

t_ch: 哈哈，爽快！就知道朋友你是个能处的~
t_en: Haha, straightforward! I knew you were someone easy to get along with!

t_ch: 算了，我没那么好奇。
t_en: Forget it. I'm not that curious.

t_ch: 唉，行吧行吧，权当是交个朋友，这条消息免费送你了……
t_en: *Sigh* Alright, alright, consider it a gesture of friendship. I'll give you this information for free...

t_ch: 喏，那个看上去一点就炸的小伙儿，家里是开杂货店的，主要营生就是给出城的探险队提供物资；
t_en: Here, that young man who looks like he might explode at any moment — his family runs a general store. Their main business is supplying expedition teams with provisions when they head out of the city.

t_ch: 至于那个看起来三天没睡的阴沉家伙，隶属于本地一个还算有些名气的探险队，他本人是队长的弟弟。
t_en: As for that gloomy guy who looks like he hasn't slept in three days, he belongs to a fairly well-known local expedition team. He's actually the younger brother of the team's leader.

t_ch: 前些日子他们小队出城探索新区域，好巧不巧遇着了黑潮，死伤那叫一个惨重。后来好不容易逃出生天，想要收拾收拾回城的时候，却发现物资出了问题。
t_en: A while ago, their squad went out to explore a new area. By sheer bad luck, they ran into the black tide, and the casualties were severe. After barely managing to escape with their lives, they tried to gather themselves and head back to the city, only to find that there was an issue with their supplies.

t_ch: 备用食物都是临期快要腐烂的就不说了，就连医疗包都是边角料凑出来的次品——显然就是出了内鬼嘛。
t_en: The backup food was all near expiry and about to spoil, and the medical kits were put together from leftover scraps — clearly, there was an inside job.

t_ch: 这两个家伙为了调查，就跑来浴场打探消息，结果得知了「那个探险队的队长前些时候与老店主达成了某项秘密交易」——这不，就吵起来了嘛~
t_en: These two guys, in order to investigate, came to the bath to gather information. They found out that "the captain of that expedition made some secret deal with the old shopkeeper some time ago" — and that's when the argument started~

t_ch: …我最后说一遍，不要再无理取闹了，动动你那木头一样的小脑袋瓜，仔细回忆一下你到底是来做什么的。
t_en: ...I'll say it one last time. Stop causing trouble. Use your wooden little brain and think carefully about what you actually came here to do.

t_ch: 我无理取闹？明明是你心虚了吧，这么就想岔开话题？想得美！
t_en: Am I being unreasonable? Clearly, it's you who's guilty and trying to change the subject! Nice try!

t_ch: 你瞧，这两个人从小一起长大，多少年的感情了，结果遇到这种事还不是奇美拉咬奇美拉——一嘴毛，哈哈~
t_en: Look at that. These two have grown up together, years of friendship, and yet when something like this happens, it's just like a chimera biting another chimera — just as messy as a mouthful of fur, haha~

t_ch: 那消息该不会是你…
t_en: Ah, that message wasn't from you, was it...

t_ch: 诶呀，我就喜欢你这样的聪明人！你也觉得他们有点吵了对不对，那不如我们来聊点别的吧。
t_en: Ah, I just love smart people like you! You think they're a bit noisy too, don't you?

t_ch: 你真的不是假面愚者吗？
t_en: Are you really not a Masked Fool?

t_ch: 假面…愚者？那是什么，听起来很有趣啊，你可以待会仔细给我讲讲吗？
t_en: The Masked... Fool? What's that? Sounds intriguing — could you tell me about it in detail later?

t_ch: 我说两位——差不多适可为止了吧？其他人可都在往这边看呢，你们确定还要继续娱乐大众吗？
t_en: I say, you two — don't you think that's enough? Everyone's staring at you now. Are you sure you want to keep this show going on?

t_ch: 哈？还不是你告诉我们——
t_en: Huh? Weren't you the one who told us —

t_ch: 你在质疑我提供的消息？
t_en: Are you questioning the information I provided?

t_ch: …他不是这个意思，但你说的「达成了某项秘密交易」实在过于暧昧不清……
t_en: ...That's not what he means. It's just that your "making a secret deal" comment is a bit too vague...

t_ch: 对呀——所以剩下的你们得自己去调查啊。难不成还要我把前因后果掰开揉碎了给你们讲清楚？那可是另外的价钱了哦。
t_en: Exactly! Which is why you'll have to figure out the rest yourselves. Or do you expect me to break it all down and explain every detail? That would cost you extra, you know.

t_ch: 你这家伙……
t_en: You...!

t_ch: 唉，算了算了，看在你们这么努力供大家消遣的份上，再追加一份赠品好了——去仔细瞧瞧探险队这几个月的医疗支出吧，运气好的话说不定能捉到只大岩鼠呢。
t_en: *Sigh* Fine, fine. Since you've worked so hard entertaining everyone here, I'll throw in a freebie — take a good look at the medical expenses for the expedition team over the past few months. If you're lucky, you might catch a big rock rat.

t_ch: 什么？利比斯叔叔不可能……
t_en: What? Uncle Libius would never...

t_ch: 我知道了，谢谢你。走吧伊菲斯，有什么事我们回去再说。
t_en: I see. Thank you. Let's go, Ifris. We'll talk later if you need.

t_ch: 多谢惠顾，欢迎下次光临~
t_en: Thank you for your visit, and we look forward to seeing you again next time~

t_ch: 好啦，吵闹的家伙已经不在了，现在我们可以好好聊聊了吧，来自「异乡」的朋友~
t_en: Alright, the noisy ones are gone now. We can have a good chat, can't we, my outlander friend~

t_ch: 「塔拉萨波利斯」，对吧？是个我从来没听说过的名字呢，原来翁法罗斯还有这样一个城邦吗？
t_en: Thalassopolis, right? I haven't heard of that name before. So, there's a city like that in Amphoreus?

t_ch: 我见过很多外乡人，也知道不少事情，但瞧瞧你——前所未见的服装样式、与众不同的言谈举止、还有……
t_en: I've seen a great many outsiders and know plenty of things, but judging from your novel appearance and unusual behavior, and...

t_ch: 哈哈，来和我讲讲你的家乡吧，朋友，我真的真的很感兴趣~
t_en: Haha, come and talk to me about your home, friend. I'm very interested.

t_ch: 嗨——朋友，我们还真是有缘啊！想要聊聊天吗？我随时奉陪哦，免费的~
t_en: Hey — friend, what a delightful coincidence it is to meet you! You wanna chat? I'll be here anytime! And for free too!

t_ch: 打探消息。
t_en: Seek information.

t_ch: 哈，想来些乐子？那你可找对人喽——放心，我手里的消息向来明码标价，绝对不坑朋友~
t_en: Hah. Wanna have some fun? Then you found the right person. Don't worry — I run an honest business. My intel is always clearly priced, and I'd never scam a friend~

t_ch: 和他聊聊。
t_en: Talk to him.

t_ch: 诶呀，你终于愿意和我聊这个了？我可是等得法吉娜都要清醒了。
t_en: Oh, you're finally willing to talk with me about this? I've been waiting for so long that Phagousa is about to wake up.

t_ch: 那么，快给我讲讲吧——你的「家乡」是个怎样的地方？你又是如何一路来此、路上可有遇到什么稀奇事？
t_en: Okay, so talk to me. What is your home like? And what did you encounter on your road here?

t_ch: 聊聊你的「家乡」。
t_en: Talk about your "home."

t_ch: 你将自己当初在黑塔空间站醒来、而后加入列车的经历「稍稍加工」了一下，讲给了眼巴巴望着你的男人。
t_en: You "modified" your experiences about waking up first in the Herta Space Station and then joining the Express to the gawking man.

t_ch: 难以想象…那个名为「黑塔」的瑟希斯眷属竟有如此伟力，能筑起如此宏大精巧的高塔，用以陈列奇珍…这番壮举怕是早已能够堪比塞勒苏斯了。
t_en: It's hard to imagine that... This Herta, kin to Cerces, possess such power, is capable of building such a large and intricate tower. And to use that as a display of her rare treasures too... I fear this feat is more than comparable to Thalesus' efforts.

t_ch: 你们那个叫「无名客」的探险队也是…以巨大的驼兽为家周游世界吗？真是浪漫啊~
t_en: Your "Nameless" exploration team... Do they also explore the world on the back of a giant beast? How romantic.

t_ch: 你果然是个有趣的人，朋友——难怪从见到你的第一眼起，我就觉得咱俩特别有缘呢。
t_en: I knew you were interesting, friend. From the moment I first laid eyes on you, I knew we had a special bond.

t_ch: 聊聊旅途经历。
t_en: Talk about your travels.

t_ch: 你将自己和同伴们这一路的开拓之旅，「略加修饰」了一下，讲给了眼前兴致盎然的男人。
t_en: You "slightly polished" your trailblazing expedition experiences with your companions and told them to the exuberant man.

t_ch: 真稀奇，你走过的那些「城邦」，我可是一个都没听说过呢。
t_en: How strange. I have never heard of any of these cities you've been to.

t_ch: 哈哈~别紧张，朋友。我没别的意思，只是些感慨罢了。
t_en: Haha, don't be nervous, friend. I don't mean anything. Just feeling a bit emotional.

t_ch: 你瞧——我虽有点小手腕，知道得比这里大部分人稍微多了那么一点点，但听完你的故事，却觉得自己现在的日子属实太过无趣啦！
t_en: Look... Though I have a little bit of skill, a little bit more capability than most people here, hearing your story makes me think that my life here is too dull!

t_ch: 真想像你一样到处旅行啊~想看看欧洛尼斯的更高处有什么、想瞧瞧塞纳托斯的冥河尽头在哪里……
t_en: I really want to adventure about like you. Want to see what waits beyond the heights of Oronyx, and where the end of the Thanatos' River of Souls lies...

t_ch: …「塔拉萨波利斯」，对吧？
t_en: ...Thalassopolis, right?

t_ch: 你们那个「探险队」还缺不缺人手？不如回程的时候也把我也捎上吧，我很想见见你们那个像兔子一样的雅努斯眷属呢~
t_en: Is your exploration team still short-handed? How about you bring me with you on the way back? I really want to see that rabbit-like kin of Janus that you mentioned.

t_ch: 你有点像某个熟人。
t_en: You remind me of someone familiar.

t_ch: 怀着微妙的心情，你给这人简单讲了讲自己与某位「寒腿叔叔」的孽缘。当然，是翁法罗斯本地化版本。
t_en: With certain strange emotions, you briefly explained your relationship with a certain Mr. Cold Feet. Of course, you gave them the localized Amphoreus version.

t_ch: 哈哈，那说不定我们真就是失散多年异父异母的亲兄弟呢~
t_en: Haha, then maybe we are long-lost brothers that share a single parent.

t_ch: 不过你们那儿的「扎格列斯信徒」可真有趣，还有那个叫「匹诺康尼」的城邦里的「酒馆」……
t_en: Though, that follower of Zagreus you talk of does sound interesting. And that tavern in the city of... Penacony, was it?

t_ch: 你不是也还没去过吗，朋友，不如下次我们一起想办法混进去瞧瞧？
t_en: You haven't been there either, right? How about next time we both find a way to get in and take a look?

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 这就要走了吗？唉，我还没聊够呢……
t_en: Leaving already? But we haven't talked enough yet...

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 我会想你的，朋友！很想很想~所以你还会来找我的，对吗？
t_en: I'll miss you, friend — so, so much. You'll come back and see me, right?

t_ch: 要上吗？要不…还是等明天吧……
t_en: Should I go in? Or maybe... maybe I'll wait until tomorrow...

t_ch: 不不不，不能有一丝怀疑…深呼吸，伊克西翁，迎接自己的宿命吧！
t_en: No, no, there can't be any doubt. Deep breath, Ixion. Embrace your destiny!

t_ch: （这个人怎么鬼鬼祟祟的，前面就是黄金裔浴池，他想干什么？）
t_en: (What is this person up to, sneaking around like that? The Chrysos Heir bath is right ahead — what does he intend to do?)
Wah—!!!

t_ch: 欸？我还以为是卫兵呢。刻法勒在上，你鬼鬼祟祟跟在我身后，想对我做什么？
t_en: Huh? I thought you were a guard. By Kephale, why are you sneaking around behind me? What are you trying to do?

t_ch: 我还想问你呢。
t_en: I should be asking you that.

t_ch: 恶人先告状是吧？
t_en: Oh, playing the victim first, are we?

t_ch: 你听好了，其实我就是预言中的十二黄金裔之一。只不过，阿格莱雅大人她们还没有找到我。
t_en: Listen closely. In fact, I am one of the Twelve Chrysos Heirs in the prophecy. It's just Lady Aglaea and the others haven't found me yet.

t_ch: 我从小便听城邦里的人提起，每位黄金裔自出生时便有征兆——我们身上都有区别于常人的独特缺陷。体现在我身上的，是语言能力的不足。
t_en: Since I was young, I have heard people in the city talk about it. Each of the Chrysos Heirs has a sign from birth — we all lack something that sets us apart from ordinary people. For me, it manifests in a deficiency in language ability.

t_ch: 有次我与邻居发生了争执，他气急败坏下，说我除了容貌俊俏外一无是处，我当即一个箭步上前拥抱了他。
t_en: One time, I had an argument with a neighbor. In a fit of anger, he said I was useless except for my good looks. I immediately took a step forward and hugged him.

t_ch: 后来我姐姐纠正我，他是在损我。可我至今仍不明白，为什么夸我帅是一种咒骂？
t_en: Later, my older sister corrected me, saying that he was insulting me. But to this day, I still don't understand — how is calling me handsome an insult?

t_ch: 是啊，为什么呢？
t_en: Yes. Why?

t_ch: 能开心活着也挺好的。
t_en: Living happily is good enough for me.

t_ch: 他们管这叫作「听不懂人话」，这不正是我身为黄金裔独有的缺陷吗？我听不懂人话是因为我的母语是神的语言，但这似乎已经超出了我身边的人的理解能力。
t_en: They call it "not understanding human language," which is precisely the flaw unique to me as a Chrysos Heir. The reason I can't understand human speech is my native language is that of the gods. However, this seems to be beyond the comprehension of those around me.

t_ch: 所以，我只好拿出一些铁证了，无论如何我都要冲进那黄金裔浴池一次。
t_en: So, I had no choice but to gather some solid evidence. No matter what, I must break into that Chrysos Heir bath at least once.

t_ch: 若我能与白厄前辈一样，沐浴在金血中而不被其侵蚀，城邦的大家才会承认我。
t_en: If I could, like senior Phainon, bathe in the golden blood without being corroded by it, then the people of the city would finally acknowledge me.

t_ch: 我欣赏你的乐观。
t_en: I admire your optimism.

t_ch: 诶，遵从既定事实怎么能算乐观呢？
t_en: Huh? How can following established facts be considered optimism?

t_ch: 我会飞，是因为我知道我能飞，而不是我相信我能飞。
t_en: I can fly because I know I can fly, not because I believe I can fly.

t_ch: 不如信我是刻法勒。
t_en: Why not just trust that I am Kephale?

t_ch: 我的神啊？！真的是您吗？您终于认可我了吗！
t_en: My god!? Is it really you? Have you finally acknowledged me!?

t_ch: 是的，我就是刻法勒。
t_en: Yes, I am Kephale.

t_ch: 明白了！您以凡人之躯行走在城邦中，这件事还不方便向世人明说。
t_en: I understand! You walk among the city's people in mortal form, and it's not yet time to reveal this truth to the world.

t_ch: 放心吧，只要您予我信心便足矣，我会用自己的力量向奥赫玛证明。
t_en: Rest assured. As long as you grant me confidence, that is enough. I will use my own strength to prove myself to Okhema.

t_ch: 不，我不是，我没有。
t_en: No, I'm not! I didn't!

t_ch: 明白了！您以凡人之躯行走在城邦中，这件事还不方便向世人明说。
t_en: I understand! You walk among the city's people in mortal form, and it's not yet time to reveal this truth to the world.

t_ch: 放心吧，只要您予我信心便足矣，我会用自己的力量向奥赫玛证明。
t_en: Rest assured. As long as you grant me confidence, that is enough. I will use my own strength to prove myself to Okhema.

t_ch: 要不先转给我<unbreak>50</unbreak>信用点？
t_en: How about sending me <unbreak>50</unbreak> credits first?

t_ch: 我的神啊，我手上没有那么多钱…不过，我会以自己的方式向你证明。
t_en: My god, I don't have that much money on me... but I will prove myself to you in my own way.

t_ch: 一股勇气涌上心头！
t_en: A surge of courage wells up within!

t_ch: 我决定了，数到1我就冲进那金血！开始——
t_en: I've decided. When I count to 1, I'll charge into that golden blood! Starting —

t_ch: <unbreak>999</unbreak>…<unbreak>998</unbreak>…<unbreak>997</unbreak>…<unbreak>996</unbreak>……
t_en: <unbreak>999</unbreak>... <unbreak>998</unbreak>... <unbreak>997</unbreak>... <unbreak>996</unbreak>...

t_ch: 面前的青年丝毫未察觉你的存在。他与身边的浴池总管激烈地争执，央求一份许可与权利。那决心就像赴死的英雄，只不过这片战地在浴场。
t_en: The young man before you has not noticed your presence at all. They are too busy locked in a ferocious argument over permissions and rights with the bath manager beside them. Their determination is like that of a dying hero, only their battlefield is actually a bathhouse.

t_ch: 孩子，你怎么又私自闯入这里？幸亏是我先发现了你，如果撞见你的是卫兵……
t_en: Child, what are you doing sneaking in here again? You're lucky it was me that found you and not one of the guards...

t_ch: 欧弥利亚婶婶，我发誓这次真的不一样了！就在今日，刻法勒已赐予我第二个征兆，虽然我答应它不能说…我是命中注定的十二黄金裔之一，你要相信我！
t_en: Auntie Aumilia, I promise this time is different! Kephale has given me a second sign today. Though I promised them I wouldn't say... I am definitely one of the twelve prophesied Chrysos Heirs. You have to believe me!

t_ch: 哎，伊克西翁，我理解你担心城邦的安危，希望尽自己所能。但万一你失败了呢？那金血，凡人碰了可是要殒命啊！
t_en: Ah, Ixion... I know that you are concerned for the safety of the city, and you want to do what you can. But what if you are unsuccessful? If a mortal comes into contact with the golden blood, they will lose their lives!

t_ch: 不可能，我就是黄金裔！况且，自我决心踏上这条道路，我便无所畏惧，就像神话中真正的英雄一样。我一定会拯救奥赫玛的诸位……
t_en: No way. I am a Chrysos Heir! Plus, I have decided to take this path for myself. So, I have no fear! Just like those heroes in the myths! I will save everyone in Okhema...

t_ch: …你有考虑过你的姐姐吗？她已经在战争中失去了父母，失去了兄长。如果你也将自己的性命置于危险中，你至少应当先与她道别，再采取行动。
t_en: ...Have you thought about your sister? She has already lost her father and mother to battle. She lost her older brother too. If you are going to put your life in danger, you should at least bid her farewell before you act.

t_ch: …我确实没有告诉她是今天。我再等等姐姐，谢谢你，欧弥利亚婶婶。
t_en: ...You're right. I didn't tell her today was the day. I will wait for her. Thank you, Auntie Aumilia.

t_ch: 等一下，路过的这位老师！
t_en: Wait a second! Hey, the master passing by!

t_ch: 我见过你！门口的那个雕像是你们造的对吧！
t_en: I've seen you before! You were the one who made that statue at the entrance!

t_ch: 什么雕像？
t_en: What statue?

t_ch: 你认错人啦。
t_en: You must have mistaken me for someone else.

t_ch: 我不可能认错！就是那个泛着蓝光的浮空旋转塑像。我亲眼看到你跟一位黑发的同伴，陪同着阿格莱雅大人，在浴场门口放下了那个物件。
t_en: I can't be mistaken! It was that floating, rotating sculpture with a bluish glow. I saw it with my own eyes — you and your black-haired companion, accompanied Lady Aglaea, placed that object there at the entrance of the baths.

t_ch: 啊，界域定锚…
t_en: Ah, the Space Anchor...

t_ch: 借浴钉矛？还真是起了个感性独特的名字…
t_en: "Space Anchor"? That's quite the poetic and unique name...

t_ch: 那其实不是雕像…
t_en: Well, it's not really a statue...

t_ch: 我知道的，那肯定是某种崭新的艺术形式，我只是暂且用「雕像」称呼它。
t_en: I knew it! It must be some cutting-edge form of artistic expression. I just called it a "statue" as a placeholder.

t_ch: 虽说浴场人来人往，空地上多个什么东西倒是不稀奇。但那作品的样貌实在太前卫了，我每次经过都忍不住多看两眼。
t_en: The baths are always crowded, so it's not unusual to find something new being placed here. But the design of that piece was so avant-garde that every time I passed by, I couldn't help but take a second look.

t_ch: 正好让我逮住你了，说什么我也得问出它的材质和制作工艺来。
t_en: Now that I've caught you, I'm determined to find out the material and craftsmanship behind it.

t_ch: 抱歉，我也不知道。
t_en: Sorry, I have no idea.

t_ch: 这你得去问我朋友…
t_en: You'd have to ask my friend...

t_ch: 这…真是太可惜了。那幽光给我的印象和永夜天帷太像，我原以为您也是一位欧洛尼斯赐福之人呢。
t_en: That's... such a pity. The eerie glow reminded me so much of the Evernight Veil. I had thought you were also someone blessed by Oronyx.

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我是这云石天宫的壁画画师，最近灵感枯竭，但此物让我重拾了创作的动力…你应该不介意我照着它画点什么吧？
t_en: To be honest, I am a mural artist for this Marmoreal Palace. Recently, I've been struggling with a lack of inspiration, but this object has reignited my creative drive... I hope you wouldn't mind if I used it as a reference for my work?

t_ch: 界域定锚拟人？
t_en: A Space Anchor personified?

t_ch: 拟人…别说，真是个好主意！画名我都想好了！就叫「旋转的异邦舞者」！
t_en: Anthropomorphizing... I must say, that's a great idea! I've even thought of a title for the piece — let's call it "The Spinning Dancer of the Outlands"!

t_ch: 行啊，画好记得给我看看。
t_en: Sure, go ahead. Show me once it's finished.

t_ch: 噢！那当然！我差点以为你会跟我收版权费呢。
t_en: Oh, absolutely! I was half expecting you to charge me a licensing fee.

t_ch: 老师你可真是个好家伙。穿搭的风格也很独特，一看就是喜欢打破规则的人。
t_en: Master, you're such a great person. Your sense of dressing style is so unique — clearly someone who enjoys breaking the rules.

t_ch: 不像那位啰嗦的{RUBY_B#浴场侍者}达罗{RUBY_E#}老兄一样——「柯珀丝女士，请不要把画板带入池中」，「柯珀丝女士，我们打烊了，请您明晰时再来」。
t_en: Unlike that talkative {RUBY_B#Baths Attendant}Daros{RUBY_E#} —"Miss Karpos, please don't bring your easel into the pool," or "Miss Karpos, we're closing now, so please return during the Lucid Hour."

t_ch: 要是脑子里全是那些条条框框，还怎么当好一个画家？老师你说是吧？
t_en: If your mind is full of those rules and restrictions, how can you be a good painter? Don't you think so, master?

t_ch: 艺术源自生活，而生活就是泡澡。只要在这浴场呆着，灵感就会像法吉娜的蜜酿一样源源不断。
t_en: Art comes from life, and life is all about soaking in the bath. As long as I'm here in the baths, inspiration will flow endlessly, just like Phagousa's honey brew.

t_ch: 但说真的，要是笔自己能画画就好了——老师，既然你来了，也给我贡献点灵感吧！
t_en: But honestly, it would be great if the brush could paint by itself — Master, since you're here, why don't you contribute some inspiration to me?

t_ch: 来聊聊？
t_en: Want to chat?

t_ch: 聊天？那太好了，只要你不介意我们聊的东西变成我下一幅作品的主题就行！
t_en: Chat? That sounds great, as long as you don't mind if what we talk about becomes the theme for my next piece!

t_ch: 你都创作过什么？
t_en: What have you created before?

t_ch: 哈哈，这谁记得清嘛，老师你肯定也不会记得自己吃过多少块面包吧。
t_en: Haha, who even remembers that? Master, you probably don't remember how many pieces of bread you've eaten either, right?

t_ch: 大多数情况下，只要我得到值得挥洒颜料的灵感，就会当场找个地方作画。有的时候如果在墙壁上难以完全表达那份触动，我就把它画在其他地方，比如瓶瓶罐罐。
t_en: In most cases, as long as I get inspiration worth putting paint to, I'll find a place to paint on the spot. Sometimes, if I can't fully express that feeling on the wall, I'll paint it somewhere else, like on bottles or jars.

t_ch: 至于创作的主题——三成泰坦传说，两成黄金裔的故事，一成日常生活，剩下的全是{RUBY_B#夜景}欧洛尼斯的帷幕{RUBY_E#}，特适合在罐子上画一周全景。
t_en: As for themes — I've done <unbreak>30%</unbreak> Titan legends, <unbreak>20%</unbreak> stories of Chrysos Heirs, <unbreak>10%</unbreak> daily life, and the rest are all {RUBY_B#night scenes} the curtains of Oronyx{RUBY_E#}, which is perfect for painting a circular view on a jar.

t_ch: …其实我家被瓶罐堆满了，这也是我天天来浴场泡着的理由…之一。
t_en: Actually, my house is full of bottles and jars. That's one of the reasons I come to the bath every day...

t_ch: 艺术能养活自己吗？
t_en: Can you make a living with art?

t_ch: 瞧你问的——肯定不能！
t_en: What a question — definitely not!

t_ch: 不过这个时期倒是例外…很多富人期待着救世后的价值震荡，他们会刻意找一些「可能会升值」的作品来收藏。
t_en: However, this period is an exception... Many wealthy individuals are anticipating the value fluctuations after salvation, and they will deliberately seek out artworks that "might appreciate in value" to collect.

t_ch: 所以要是缺利衡币，我就专画黄金裔和他们的逐火之旅，报酬刚好够我买新颜料的开销——你是不知道那些好用的颜料到底有多贵，有些还得从大地兽的碗里抢。
t_en: So if I'm short on Balance Coins, I'll just focus on painting the Chrysos Heirs and their Flame-Chase Journey. The payment will be enough to cover the cost of new paints — you have no idea how expensive those good paints are. Some even have to be taken from the bowls of dromases.

t_ch: 不过，最近元老院的人会委托我创作一些宣传性质的东西，他们给得相当多，但……
t_en: However, recently, the Council of Elders has been commissioning me to create some promotional works. They pay quite generously, but...

t_ch: …那些人眼中的「原创艺术」更类似于一种「工具」，每次给他们画东西都让我郁闷透了。所以我就从废稿堆里随便抽一张出来，随便加两笔就交过去。
t_en: ...In their eyes, "original art" is more like a "tool." Every time I paint something for them, it just depresses me. So I just grab a random sketch from the pile of discarded drafts, add a few strokes, and hand it over.

t_ch: 他们喜欢，我心态稳定，双赢。
t_en: They like it, and I stay calm. It's a win-win.

t_ch: 最近在画什么？
t_en: What have you been painting lately?

t_ch: 最近？主要是和一位老前辈合作，进行壁画修缮…量又大，还遍布城内各处，要不是因为憧憬那位前辈，这工作我都不想接。
t_en: Lately? Mostly collaborating with an old senior to restore murals... It's a big job, spread all over the city. If I weren't so admiring of that senior, I wouldn't even want to take on this work.

t_ch: 不止这个原因…偷偷告诉你。每次壁画进行修缮，负责的艺术家都会添上一两笔自己的东西。你可能觉得没什么，但经不起积少成多。举个例子——
t_en: But that's not the only reason... I'll tell you a secret. Every time a mural is restored, the artist in charge adds a few strokes of their own. You might think it's nothing, but small changes add up over time. For example —

t_ch: 要是我告诉你，一千个光历年来，城门口那些吉奥里亚的图样，实际上最早是艾格勒的故事，你能想象吗？
t_en: If I told you that the symbols of Georios at the city gates, which have been there for a thousand Light Calendar years, were originally the story of Aquila, could you imagine that?

t_ch: 一想到神谕和史书可能也因类似情况一步步变得面目全非，我就感到有些哀伤。真的是一点兴致都提不起来。
t_en: Just the thought that prophecies and historical records might have gradually become distorted due to similar circumstances makes me feel a bit sorrowful. Honestly, it dampens any enthusiasm I have.

t_ch: 下次再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 好，慢走…我再泡一个时刻也该出去了…不，或许两个…？
t_en: Alright, take care... I should probably head out after soaking for a little longer... no, maybe two more rounds...?

t_ch: 下次再聊吧。
t_en: Let's catch up another time.

t_ch: 好，慢走…我再泡一个时刻也该出去了…不，或许两个…？
t_en: Alright, take care... I should probably head out after soaking for a little longer... no, maybe two more rounds...?

t_ch: …讨论会就快结束了，要不要再多吃点？
t_en: ...The discussion will be wrapping up soon. Want to have some more to eat?

t_ch: 难得有随便享用浴场餐品的机会，不能浪费，得值回我为那几篇论文花费的心血！
t_en: It's rare to have the chance to enjoy the baths' food. Can't waste it, and gotta make sure it's worth the effort I put into those papers!

t_ch: 你在念叨什么呢？
t_en: What are you mumbling about?

t_ch: …你是谁？我先警告你，偷听是违反塔兰顿律法的行为，小心把你给举报了！
t_en: ...Who are you? I'll warn you first, eavesdropping is a violation of Talanton's law, so be careful or I'll report you!

t_ch: …细说心血？
t_en: ...What effort?

t_ch: 就是占用了我一刻钟进餐的时间，胡乱编出来的文章…等等，你又是谁？
t_en: Just the paper article I quickly put together during a 1-quint meal break... Wait, who are you again?

t_ch: 我警告你，偷听是违反塔兰顿律法的行为，小心我把你给举报了！
t_en: I'm warning you, eavesdropping is against Talanton's law. Watch out, I might report you!

t_ch: 要是想指责我不务正业，骂我偷懒，那我可必须得说——你来错地方了。云石天宫是给大家放松的场所，不是认真工作的地。
t_en: If you're here to criticize me for not being productive and call me lazy, I must say — you've come to the wrong place. The Marmoreal Palace is a place for everyone to relax, not for serious work.

t_ch: 就跟你说到这里，没看见我正忙着呢。忙着享受生活也是在忙！
t_en: I've said enough. Can't you see I'm busy? Enjoying life is still a form of work!

t_ch: 什么事…怎么还是你啊？
t_en: What is it... why is it you again?

t_ch: 嗨，正好我刚想出在下次讨论会上发表的观点，心情大好，陪你简单聊聊也不是不行。
t_en: Hey, I just figure out the point I can present at the next discussion. I'm in a good mood, so I don't mind chatting with you a little.

t_ch: 当然，什么时候结束，由我说了算。
t_en: Of course, when it ends is up to me.

t_ch: 你到底在这做什么？
t_en: What exactly are you doing here?

t_ch: 看不出来吗？我正在进行学术研究，是伟大的真理追寻者！
t_en: Can't you see? I'm conducting academic research — I'm a seeker of the great truth!

t_ch: 大家不是都说，泡浴有助于放松思想，充足的糖分能帮助大脑高速运转。所以，如你所见，我正在浴池的餐桌旁思考。不觉得是个很高效的做法吗？
t_en: Everyone says that soaking helps relax the mind, and plenty of sugar helps the brain work at high speed. So, as you can see, I'm thinking while sitting at the bath's dining table. Don't you think it's an efficient way to do things?

t_ch: 边进行工作，边补充营养。要是那些学者也能像我这样钻研问题，早就有突破性的进展了，还至于每次都在讨论会上争辩那几个话题？
t_en: While working, I also make sure to replenish my energy. If those scholars could dive into their problems like I do, there'd have been breakthroughs by now — why do they always end up debating the same old topics at every conference?

t_ch: 吃饱了不会发困吗？
t_en: Aren't you tired after eating?

t_ch: 发困不是好事吗？梦境是灵感的源头，能给人许多启发。我还得虔诚地感谢塞纳托斯，每次都让我睡得那么舒服呢。
t_en: Isn't feeling sleepy a good thing? Dreams are the source of inspiration and can offer many insights. I have to sincerely thank Thanatos for always making my sleep so comfortable.

t_ch: 所以你是在划水吧…
t_en: So you're just slacking off, huh...

t_ch: 喂，注意你的言辞！思考需要充分的闲暇，我是在厚积薄发，懂吗？
t_en: Hey, mind your words! Thought requires plenty of leisure. I'm accumulating energy for a sudden burst, you know?

t_ch: 算了，跟你说这些也没用。果然还是独自吃饭的时间最快乐了……
t_en: Forget it. There's no point explaining this to you. After all, the happiest time is when I eat alone...

t_ch: 你参加的是什么讨论会？
t_en: What discussion are you attending?

t_ch: 都是些很无聊的内容，最近讨论的主题是：由古生物的生命信息拼合得到的奇美拉，算是新物种吗？你说，是不是没事找事？
t_en: It's all really boring stuff. The recent topic was: Are chimeras, created from the combined life information of ancient creatures, a new species? Don't you think that's just making a problem out of nothing?

t_ch: 还鼓励所有成员都去和奇美拉来个近距离接触。哎，为了完成指标，我也当上了某只奇美拉小队的管理员，必须得监督它们工作，真是浪费时间。
t_en: They even encourage all members to get up close and personal with the chimeras. *sigh* In order to meet the quotas, I've ended up as the administrator of a chimera squad. I have to supervise their work, which is such a waste of time.

t_ch: 要是有这个闲工夫，还不如让我多泡会澡，指不定真能想出点什么来呢！
t_en: If I had this free time, I'd rather soak in the bath longer — who knows, maybe I'd come up with something!

t_ch: 就不能拒绝吗？
t_en: Can't you just refuse?

t_ch: …拒绝？要是不参加这个研讨会，我去哪找这么多免费的吃的？
t_en: ...Refuse? If I don't attend this seminar, where else can I get all this free food?

t_ch: 要是不做这个奇美拉管理员，不完成生命花园的指标，我该怎么说服负责人放我随意使用浴池，还能领花园的津贴？
t_en: If I didn't take on this role as the chimera administrator and didn't meet the Garden of Life's quotas, how would I convince the person in charge to let me use the baths freely and still receive the Garden's allowance?

t_ch: 拒绝是不可能的，最多就是用行动抗议罢了——告诉所有人，我不想干！
t_en: Refusing is impossible. At most, I'll protest through my actions — let everyone know I don't want to do it!

t_ch: 但我也不辞职，让他们去急。嘿嘿。
t_en: But I'm not resigning. Let them worry about it. Heh heh.

t_ch: 就不能好好工作吗？
t_en: Can't you just work properly?

t_ch: 面前的男人用种极度不可置信的眼神望着你，仿佛你说出了什么禁忌的言论。
t_en: The man in front of you looks at you with an expression of extreme disbelief, as though you've said something forbidden.

t_ch: …离谱。我不想和你说话了。
t_en: ...Ridiculous. I don't want to talk to you anymore.

t_ch: 凭什么由你说了算？
t_en: Why should it be up to you?

t_ch: 行啊，没问题，由你说了算！那就聊到这里，我还正愁找不到借口打发你走呢！
t_en: Alright, fine, it's your call! Let's end the conversation here. I was actually looking for an excuse to send you on your way!

t_ch: 你好，年轻人，欢迎来到云石天宫的书库…记得找书前先去把手擦干。翻阅可以随便找个墙边一靠，若是要借出…去找那边的阿波罗尼娅，填个调书单。
t_en: Hello, youngster, welcome to the repository of the Marmoreal Palace... Remember to wipe your hands before looking for any books. You can browse freely while leaning against any wall, but if you wish to borrow something, go find Apollonia over there and fill out a book request form.

t_ch: 这些不是卷轴吗？
t_en: Aren't these scrolls?

t_ch: 哈哈，这倒是常听人问起，但要知道，卷轴也好纸张也罢，都只是一种形式，凡是载有学识的媒介，在我们这统一都可称做「书」——包括字画和石版，均在馆藏范围内。
t_en: Haha, that's a question I hear quite often. But you see, whether it's a scroll or paper, they're just forms. Any medium that carries knowledge is collectively referred to as a "book" here — this includes calligraphy, paintings, and even steles, all of which are part of our collection.

t_ch: 真敢在水边放书啊？
t_en: You really dare place books by the water?

t_ch: 哈哈，不必担心，此处的识本都受了瑟希斯的庇佑，哪怕书页被整个濡湿，也绝无损毁之忧…不过，污渍倒是防不住的，所以再重申一下，记得擦干手。
t_en: Haha, no need to worry. The texts here are all blessed by Cerces, so even if the pages get completely soaked, there's no risk of them being damaged... However, stains are another matter, so let me remind you once again — make sure to dry your hands!

t_ch: 你若是想随便翻翻，二柜到四柜的哲史典籍会是个不错的选择。数算在七柜，故事诗章在十五柜。另外，年轻人可能会喜欢二十柜以后的故事书…
t_en: If you're just browsing, the philosophical and historical texts in cabinets two to four would be a good choice. For math and numerical works, try cabinet seven. The narrative poems are in cabinet fifteen. Additionally, young people might enjoy the storybooks in cabinets after twenty...

t_ch: 看花了，有什么推荐吗？
t_en: I'm feeling overwhelmed. Do you have any recommendations?

t_ch: 感觉都不太感兴趣…
t_en: I'm not interested in any of those...

t_ch: 嗯…看你的打扮是来自外乡，一定还没适应这边的环境吧？这本或许能让你对奥赫玛的印象更加轻松些。这是拓本，无需归还，拿着慢慢读吧。
t_en: Well... judging by your attire, you must be from out of town and haven't quite adjusted to the environment here yet? This book might make your impression of Okhema a bit more lighthearted. It's a rubbing, so you don't need to return it. Take it and read at your own pace.

t_ch: 在这末世中，也就只有书卷的清新味道能带给人些安宁…哈哈，安宁得都忘了自我介绍了。
t_en: In this post-apocalyptic world, the fresh scent of books is about the only thing that brings peace... Haha, I've gotten so caught up that I forgot to introduce myself.

t_ch: 我是书库的负责人之一，名叫泽诺托斯。在这书库中遇到的问题，都可以来找我，只要在馆内别大声喧哗，吵到别人就好。
t_en: I am one of the managers of the repository, and my name is Zenotus. Any issues you encounter here, feel free to come to me. Just make sure not to raise your voice too loudly within the repository and disturb others.

t_ch: 流水声不会打扰默想，反倒使思维更加清晰……
t_en: The sound of flowing water doesn't interrupt quiet contemplation. It actually clears the mind...

t_ch: 如何，年轻人？找到想看的书了吗？
t_en: So, youngster, have you found a book you like?

t_ch: 有空聊聊吗？
t_en: Got a moment to chat?

t_ch: 哈哈，到我这个年纪，最不缺的就是时间。你说吧，年轻人。
t_en: Haha, at my age, the one thing I have plenty of is time. Go ahead, speak your mind, youngster.

t_ch: 讲讲这里的馆藏？
t_en: Can you tell me about the collection here?

t_ch: 在这奥赫玛能寻见的书，就都能在这图书馆里寻见。不是老头子我吹嘘，哪怕树庭的圣树里刻下的典籍，都不一定有这全。
t_en: Any book you can find in Okhema, you can find here in this repository. I'm not bragging, but even the scriptures carved into the sacred trees of the Grove may not have as complete a collection as we do here.

t_ch: 若是不信，你可以看看离我们最近的第一个书架，百余本全是馆藏目录。多亏我们一位同僚发明出了「分类编号法」，不然想找本书，简直难如登天。
t_en: If you don't believe me, you can take a look at the first bookshelf nearest to us. Our catalog alone is over a hundred books thick. We're lucky that one of our colleagues invented the classification numbering system, or else finding a book here would be as difficult as climbing into the sky.

t_ch: 其中，泰坦祭仪的述章居多，新人祭司们为通过教派的测验，往往一到欢喜月就会来集中借阅一番，那也是我们最忙的时候。
t_en: Among them, the majority are texts on Titan rituals. New priests, eager to pass their sect's trials, often come to focus on borrowing these during the Month of Joy. That's when we're busiest.

t_ch: 其次是医典、哲章、史记…同类书目彼此间多有矛盾之处，借阅的人几乎都是为了给辩论添点物证才会借下。
t_en: Next are the medical compendiums, philosophical texts, and historical records... there are many contradictions between these similar books, and most people borrow them to add evidence for their debates.

t_ch: 最后才是游记与创想故事，年轻人的最爱，我这老头子也读得进去。
t_en: Lastly, there are travelogues and imaginative stories, which are youngsters' favorites. Even an old man like me can get into them.

t_ch: 说来，最里面的架子还收了不少浴场客人自己的手稿，甚至还有些账本什么的——至于内容嘛，见仁见智了。
t_en: By the way, the innermost shelves hold many manuscripts from the bath guests themselves, even some account books... as for the content, well, it's all up to personal interpretation.

t_ch: 这图书馆的历史…
t_en: The history of this repository...

t_ch: 我猜你是想问，为什么会在云石天宫里建个图书馆，是吧？嗯…这要扯到几十个光历年前——记得刚好是第四十三个奥赫玛百年祭那会。
t_en: I guess you're wondering why there's a repository in the Marmoreal Palace, right? Well... this goes back several Light Calendar years ago — exactly during the <unbreak>43</unbreak>rd Okhema Centennial Festival.

t_ch: 那年，浴场刚刚完成它有史以来规模最大的一次全面修缮，整个光鲜亮丽了许多。但人们有所不知，那同时代表建筑师们都要没活可做了，我也是其中之一。
t_en: That year, the bath had just finished its largest renovation in history, becoming much more splendid. But what people didn't know was that this also meant the architects would be out of work, and I was one of them.

t_ch: 我的老朋友们担心之后的营生，我嘛，运气好些，利衡无忧，就担心起这门学问失传，一合计，把浴场的闲置区域改造了一番，成了个谁都能用的小教学室。
t_en: My old friends were worried about their future livelihoods, but I, being luckier and with a steady balance of wealth, only worried about this knowledge being lost. So, after some discussion, we decided to repurpose an unused area of the baths into a small classroom that anyone could use.

t_ch: 一开始，学者们也被吸引来讲学，他们四处搜集教材，用完了就堆在架子上。久了，来上课的人，反倒被来看书的人嫌吵，两边闹了几个光历年，书是越堆越多。
t_en: At first, scholars were attracted to teach here. They gathered textbooks from all over and piled them on the shelves once they were done using them. Over time, those attending the classes became annoyed by the noise from those just coming to read, and the arguments went on for several Light Calendar years, with more and more books piling up.

t_ch: 最后，是缇宝大人她们动用权能，把遗邦的识本全部搜集了来，在公民大会过了个表决，这地方被冠了个「学识枢纽」名头…而我这些老伙计，自然跟着不愁营生了。
t_en: Finally, Lady Tribbie and the others used their powers to gather all the knowledge from the fallen nations. After a vote in the citizens' assembly, this place was given the title of 'Knowledge Hub'... and my old companions no longer had to worry about our livelihoods.

t_ch: 最繁闹那阵，这书库甚至年年举办「觅宝活动」，自从黑潮的情况愈加严重之后，这传统就暂停了。等黄金裔大人完成神谕之后，说不定还能再办上几场。
t_en: During the busiest times, this repository even held an annual "Treasure Hunt" event. However, since the situation with the black tide has worsened, that tradition has been paused. Once the Chrysos Heir completes the prophecy, perhaps a few more events will be held again.

t_ch: 有什么烦心事吗？
t_en: Is there something troubling you?

t_ch: 烦心…倒算不上，最多算是为时代变迁而叹上几口气罢了…
t_en: Troubling... not really. It's more like sighing at the changes of the times...

t_ch: 好些客人跟我提议，希望我能帮他们拓些新书看看，但他们口中的「新书」，多是些幻想曲、情爱诗。以往的哲史与数算类目，却越来无人问津。
t_en: Many guests have suggested that I help them acquire some new books, but the "new books" they're referring to are mostly fantasy tunes and love poetry. Meanwhile, the older categories like philosophy, history, and mathematics are becoming increasingly neglected.

t_ch: 其实这倒无妨，黄金裔们骁勇善战，人们对生活有了热情，应是好事。烦心的是这帮人身上的共性——把看书视作消遣，走马观花不说，还经常边吃边看。
t_en: Actually, it's not a big deal. The Chrysos Heirs are brave and skilled warriors, and the enthusiasm people have for life is a good thing. What bothers me, though, is the common trait among these people — they treat reading as mere entertainment. Not only do they skim through without paying attention, but they often eat while reading as well.

t_ch: 最近，他们甚至怂恿我在这书库四周辟出个角落当做饮食区，提供躺食的餐点…哈哈，虽说这要求其实还算合理，但我这心里总也有块疙瘩。
t_en: Recently, they've even urged me to set up a corner around this repository as a dining area, offering meals that can be eaten while lying down... Haha, though this request seems reasonable enough, I can't shake the discomfort in my heart.

t_ch: 也不知是该怪他们特立独行，还是该夸他们懂得生活了。谁让这旁边就杵着全翁法罗斯最好的休憩处呢？
t_en: I don't know whether I should blame them for being so unconventional, or praise them for knowing how to enjoy life. After all, right next door is the best resting place in all of Amphoreus.

t_ch: 不多打扰了。
t_en: I won't disturb you any longer.

t_ch: 没事，其实比起和我这种老头聊天，书里能学到的东西更多…闲着就去转转吧。
t_en: It's fine. Actually, there's more to learn from books than from chatting with an old man like me... Feel free to wander around when you have time.

t_ch: 不多打扰了。
t_en: I won't disturb you any longer.

t_ch: 没事，其实比起和我这种老头聊天，书里能学到的东西更多…闲着就去转转吧。
t_en: It's fine. Actually, there's more to learn from books than from chatting with an old man like me... Feel free to wander around when you have time.

t_ch: 这是个远离喧嚣的好地方——没有了浴池人群的烦躁，高温蒸汽带来的晕眩感也在清爽的凉风中尽数消散，无名的虫类在低唱，瀑布述说着不可解的预言。
t_en: This is a great place away from the bustle — none of the clamoring bath crowds, and the faintness brought about by the hot steam vanishes with the cool breeze. Nameless bugs trill in low song, with the waterfall whispering inscrutable prophecies.

t_ch: 所有景象都令你感到分外舒适，但仍有些美中不足——这里实在太过安静了。
t_en: The sights fill you with particular comfort, though it still seems like something is amiss — it's too quiet here.

t_ch: …水漫琴？我见过，这是卡弥特斯的特色乐器。根据记载，卡弥特斯是座受墨涅塔赐福的遥远城邦，那里的居民个个都是极具天赋的艺术家。
t_en: ...A water lyre? I've seen it before — it's a signature instrument of Carmitis. According to records, Carmitis was a distant city blessed by Mnestia. All her residents were artistic prodigies.

t_ch: 可惜，卡弥特斯在很久以前就消失了，连同那些珍贵的、早已失传的技艺。这座风琴摆在这许久了，始终未能找到称职的演奏者。
t_en: Alas, Carmitis disappeared a long time ago, along with those precious arts long lost to time. This water lyre has been here for a long time, unable to secure a worthy musician to play it.

t_ch: 美好的事物容易被摧毁，艺术总是伴随着苦难。墨涅塔的祝福，也是诅咒啊。
t_en: Beautiful things are destroyed easily, and art is always accompanied by misery. Mnestia's blessing is also a curse.
